events.summary: A Borg sphere from 2373 tries to stop first contact; the Enterprise-E destroys it and works to restore history.
Debris from the Borg sphere, destroyed by the Enterprise-E, impacts on Earth near the North Pole. The debris remained undiscovered until 2153.
In an alternate timeline the Borg assimilate Earth, leaving nine billion drones by the late 24th century.
Lieutenant Hawk is assimilated and later blown clear of the ship by Worf.

Zefram Cochrane launches the Phoenix, mankind's first manned warp-capable ship, from Bozeman, Montana with Commander William Riker and Lt. Commander Geordi La Forge as passengers from the Enterprise-E. Its brief warp flight is detected by a Vulcan survey ship, the T'Plana-Hath, passing through the local sector, and the ship changes course to investigate. This leads to the historic First Contact between Humans and extraterrestrial life (of public record, as earlier unofficial and unbeknownst contacts had been made).

Zefram Cochrane launches the Phoenix, mankind's first manned warp-capable ship, from a remote location in the continental United States of America. Its brief warp flight is detected by a Vulcan survey ship, the T'Plana-Hath, passing through the local sector, and the ship changes course to investigate. This leads to the historic First Contact between Humans and extraterrestrial life. Upon its landing, Cochrane killed the first Vulcan to set foot on Earth. This action resulted in the early expansion of the Terran Empire, allowing Humans to drastically increase their technological capability and begin a campaign of warfare and conquest. The day was later celebrated as a holy day by the Terran Empire.
Approximate – Humans launch the first warp ships to explore the galaxy.

episodes.title: Where No One Has Gone Before

events.summary: On Earth, Zefram Cochrane mentions details about First Contact with the Vulcans on April 5, 2063 in a commencement address at Princeton, revealing what "really" happened during the events leading up to First Contact. He claimed that it involved "a group of cybernetic creatures from the future" who attempted to prevent the successful warp flight of the Phoenix and whose ultimate goal was the enslavement of Humanity. According to Cochrane, they were defeated by a group of Humans who were also from the future and who had seemingly followed them back through time. However, his remarks were largely shrugged off by his audience given his tendency toward imaginative stories and intoxication and Cochrane recanted his own statements several years later.
The SS Valiant is launched.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: A small band of Ba'ku flee their dying system and settle a world remote from any conflict.
Astronomer John Burke maps an area of space, including Sherman's Planet.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: The deep space probe Friendship 1 is launched from Earth.
The Great Awakening on Argelius II takes place, turning its population from a violent to a placid society.
The Draco lizard became extinct by this year.
Approximate – The Emperor of the Klingon Empire dies without any successors claiming the throne. This results in the Klingon High Council taking effective political control of the Empire.
At the peak of their empire, a Skorr philosopher named Alar starts the process of reforming his society into a nation of pacifists.
Intersat code, a form of interstellar communication for Earth spaceships, is discontinued.
The Earth colony ship SS Conestoga is launched with the mission of colonizing the planet Terra Nova.
The last of the Kzinti Wars ends around this year with a Human victory.
Deron Warner is born in Tycho City, Luna.
Ginger Summers dies.
Janice Lee Raymond is born in Chicago, Illinois.
Marvin Hildebrandt is born in New York City, New York.
Sometime around this year, a group of Ocampa are taken away from their homeworld and system with Suspiria in search of "more interesting places". In this year, they settle on a station.
The Q known as Quinn is imprisoned inside a rogue comet by the Q Continuum after he expresses a desire to end his existence.
Francis M. Raymond dies.
Kelleye N. Raymond dies.
Marina S. Despina dies.
Mary A. Grant dies.
Zefram Cochrane offers his much-quoted advice against trying to be a great man.

According to Rom, Slug-o-Cola and its advertising slogan ("Drink Slug-o-Cola.The slimiest cola in the galaxy.") have been around at least this long, as he did not believe they had changed their slogan in nearly three hundred years.

In an alternate timeline, Molly O'Brien is sent to this time on Golana from three hundred years in the future. One version of Molly spends ten years in this time period before being retrieved and returned to her own time. This timeline is erased when the eighteen-year old Molly sends her younger self back to her own time.
The Vulcan Vanik joins the Vulcan Space Program.
Bernadette Fuller is born.
On Tarakis, a planet in the Delta Quadrant, eighty-two civilian Nakan are massacred by a military force led by Commander Saavdra.
The nomadic Moneans discover the Waters. By the 2370s, they have built up a permanent home here, with a population of 80,000 and numerous oxygen refineries.
June sup: The SS Conestoga reaches Terra Nova and founds the Terra Nova colony.
By this year, the post-atomic horror is underway on Earth with all "United Earth nonsense" being abolished and the New United Nations ceasing to exist.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: In this year, the number of stars on the flag of the United States of America changes from the 52 displayed since 2033.
The Earth era known as AD is still in effect as of this year.
Around this year, the use of solar panels become far more common than they were in the past. They were used on everything from toasters to trucks. )
Stephen Richey dies on the planet Theta VIII. His remains are discovered by Commander William T. Riker, Lt. Commander Data, and Lt. Worf of the USS Enterprise in 2365.

Earth's Space Agency plans to send a second wave of two hundred colonists to the Terra Nova colony. Mark Logan leads the opposition to this plan of the arrival of more colonists, even threatening to use force against them. After a series of hostile transmissions, contact with the colony ceases. Soon after, the colony is destroyed by an asteroid impact, scattering toxic debris over the hemisphere. The fate of the colony became a mystery on Earth for decades.
Brent S. Jay Raymond dies.
On Acamar III, a blood feud between the Lornak and Tralesta clans begins, which will eventually end two hundred years later with the near extinction of the Tralesta.
T'Pol is born on the planet Vulcan.
Tommy Raymond dies.
Wil W. Lachance Raymond dies.
The Orpheus Mining Authority is also established along with colony.
The Orpheus Mining Complex on Luna is established by John Frederick Paxton's father.
By this year, Tret's people had lost tens of millions of people to the Loque'eque virus.
Under the terms of the Treaty of 2097, the Andorian class D planetoid Weytahn is officially claimed by the Vulcans, who call it Paan Mokar.
The Nexus ribbon is reckoned to have passed through the galaxy this year.
Kolos begins serving as an advocate within the Klingon Empire.
Soval fires a weapon for the last time until 2152.
The Earth survey ship SS Hokule'a is launched for deep space exploration.
The ECS Horizon, an Earth J-class cargo starship, is commissioned.
The first Human colony on the planet Mars is established, marking the birth of the Martian colonies.
Around this time, the Andorians established contact with the Aenar, previously only known to the Andorians as creatures from ancient myths and stories.
Shortly before or in this year, a Vulcan science vessel surveys Berengaria VII, describing its dragon-like reptiles as over two hundred meters in length and breathing fire.
The Earth colony ship Yuri Gagarin is launched.
The Redjac entity, incarnated as "Jack the Ripper", kills eight women at the Martian colonies.
The Taurean system is briefly explored and a spaceship is lost near there. Over the next 164 years, a ship will disappear in this region exactly every 27.346 years.
Gralik Durr becomes the primary technician of the kemocite refining facility on a Xindi-Arboreal colony.
Jonathan Archer is born in Upstate New York on Earth.
Lela is born on Trill.
Vulcans accuse Andorians of an incursion on Weytahn (which the Vulcans call Paan Mokar) and violation of the Treaty of 2097.
By now Earth has done away with war, poverty and disease, and their causes.
Kiki Morris Raymond dies.
Chris Tezber Raymond dies.
Doctor Phlox becomes a physician.
The Earth starship SS Tomobiki is launched.

The Warp Five Complex is officially opened by Zefram Cochrane as part of the Warp Five program. During his dedication ceremony speech, he utters the phrase "This engine will let us go boldly, where no man has gone before", variations of which will adorn the dedication plaques of several future vessels.
Craig Weiss Raymond dies.
On his eighth birthday, Jonathan Archer receives a copy of The Cosmos A to Z from his father Henry Archer. Jonathan spends hours staring at its front cover depicting the Arachnid Nebula.
The Earth survey ships HMS Lord Nelson and SS Seattle are launched.
Wade Felker Raymond dies.
Charles Tucker III born in Panama City, Florida.
Mesrop Abrahamian is born on Mars.
Together with his father Henry Archer, nine-year-old Jonathan builds a model spaceship.
Henry Archer starts suffering from Clarke's Disease.
In 2022, La'An Noonien-Singh told a version of James T. Kirk from an alternate future that the photonic bomb was invented about a century after that time. That would put it circa 2122.
T'Pau is born on Vulcan.

The Vulcan Ministry of Security sends 109 surgically altered Vulcan operatives to Agaron to infiltrate various criminal organizations in order to topple them. However, Menos, Jossen, and seventeen other operatives do not return afterward.
Liana, the daughter of Ezral and Maya, is born.
The Advanced Weather Control Station opens.
the colony ship SS Mariposa is launched from Earth's Baikonur Cosmodrome on a colonization mission to the Ficus sector.
The European Hegemony introduces a new distress beacon for starships working for this Earth-based alliance.

According to Benjamin Sisko, this was the approximate year that Earth's society underwent massive changes compared to a hundred years earlier. These changes were still present in society at least until the 24th century.
Henry Archer, father of Jonathan, dies of Clarke's Disease.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Travis Mayweather born aboard the ECS Horizon, between Draylax and the Vega colony.
Keven Scotti Raymond dies.
Birth of Patricia F. O'Malley.
T'Pol's teeth are sealed with a trifluorinate compound.
Trip Tucker's mother buys a copy of Emory Erickson: Father of the Transporter for her seven-year-old son. For the next month, Trip makes her read it to him every night before bed.
entities: Emory Erickson: Father of the Transporter

events.summary: A Kantare supply ship crash lands on an uninhabited planet halfway between Kantare and Kotara Barath stranding its only two survivors, Ezral and Liana, on the planet's surface for the next twenty-two years.
Hoshi Sato is born in Kyoto, Japan.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Nicki Kreitzman dies.
Sarah Paul Raymond dies.
A starship disappears near the Taurean region.
In a dream Jonathan Archer has in 2152, a dog named Duffy is born this year.
Kolos begins his service as an advocate on Narendra III.
The Paraagan mining colony on Paraagan II is established by a group of thirty miners.
A Klingon vessel emerges from the Delphic Expanse with its crew being anatomically inverted, but still alive.
Dr. Arik Soong steals a number of Augment embryos from Cold Station 12..

Jonathan Archer, as a senior undergraduate, competes – probably for Stanford University – in the 2134 North American Water Polo Regionals against Princeton University. His team's last-minute victory ensures them a place in the finals of the same tournament.
The Earth starship HMS New Zealand is launched.

Working for the Ministry of Security, T'Pol tracks down the fugitive Vulcan operatives Menos and Jossen to Risa and pursues them into the planet's jungles. She kills Jossen (thinking he was reaching for a weapon) but Menos escapes.

At age 24, and in flight school in San Francisco, Jonathan Archer meets Margaret Mullin, with whom he becomes romantically involved. The night before he graduates, he asked her to marry him. She turns him down, as she does not want to become a "Starfleet widow."
The Vulcan Captain Vanik assumes command of the starship Ti'Mur.
Syrran discovers a katric ark on Vulcan containing the katra of Surak, and takes Surak's katra into his own mind.
The Earth starship SS Buckaroo Banzai is launched.
Another estimated passage of the Nexus through the galaxy.
Yolen starts his studies in geology.
Charles Tucker III joins Starfleet.

Emory Erickson attempts to top his earlier achievement by inventing a sub-quantum teleportation device, which was intended to make his transporter obsolete. However, his invention is a failure when he loses his son in subspace.
Hoshi Sato has a pen pal from Brisbane, Australia.
The ECS Horizon encounters a group of stranded Deltans. Fifteen-year-old Travis Mayweather finds them very attractive and very open, so he tries to overcome his feelings with a hard workout.
Commander Shran assumes command of the Andorian Imperial Warship Kumari.
Wendy Ruiz Raymond dies.
A group of V'tosh ka'tur, Vulcans who wished to no longer suppress their emotions, leave Vulcan aboard the Vahklas under the command of Captain Tavin.
Arik Soong is raising his group of Augment children on Trialas IV during this time.
Jonathan Archer and Charles Tucker III meet for the first time.
Phlox and his son Mettus stop speaking to each other over a disagreement on Denobulan prejudices about Antarans.

The NX Program breaks the warp 2 barrier with the NX-Alpha pathfinder starship. Piloted by A.G. Robinson, NX-Alpha breaks apart after it reaches warp 2.2. Shortly after, Jonathan Archer and Robinson achieve warp 2.5 with NX-Beta on an unauthorized flight. The NX prototypes are grounded for three months after the incident.
Arik Soong is found and captured, leaving the Augments he was raising by themselves in the Trialas system.
The Cabal begins a war against the Tandarans. All Suliban are subsequently rounded up and placed in detention camps, even those not in the Cabal.
Duvall becomes the first Human to break warp 3 in the NX-Delta.
In a dream Jonathan Archer has, a dog named Duffy dies this year.
The Borg discover the existence of the Omega molecule.
The Earth ship SS Urusei Yatsura is launched.
Marvin Hildebrandt dies.
Starfleet begins research on force fields.
The Omega training mission is held on Titan; Jonathan Archer and Charles Tucker III participate. Archer saves Tucker's life during a malfunction with his environmental suit.
The verteron array is used to direct Comet Burke on a course to impact the Martian North Pole in January 2155.
Phlox sees his second wife, Feezal, for the last time, until they both attend a medical conference on Dekendi III in 2152.
A horse of good stock is born in the Skagaran colony.
According to Garos, he led a survey mission to study the Akaali this year.
Doctor Phlox attends an Interspecies Medical Exchange conference on Tiburon. The Klingon doctor Antaak also attends the conference, disguised as a member of the Mazarite delegation.
Emory Erickson first attempts to re-materialize his son Quinn aboard his research ship.
Harris quits his service at Starfleet Security.
On Valakis, twelve million Valakians die this year due to a genetic disease.
Patricia F. O'Malley graduates from Starfleet Academy.
T'Pol begins working at the Vulcan compound in Sausalito on Earth.
T'Pol takes a five-day leave and visits Carbon Creek, Pennsylvania.
Doctor Phlox begins living on Earth in San Francisco, California.
Earth becomes fully unified when all nation-states join the World Government, completing a process initiated earlier in the century by the European Hegemony.
February – Matthew Ryan had his last steak until he ate another one in August 2151.
Seeking out medical aid by more advanced species, the Valakians launch four sub-warp ships.
The Akaali apothecary Riaan learned the first time about the epidemic disease spread among her people.

The keel of Enterprise NX-01, Earth's first warp 5-capable starship, is laid. In October, Jonathan Archer is selected as her captain over A.G. Robinson by Admiral Maxwell Forrest, despite Gardner having been considered a more suitable choice by Ambassador Soval.
Three Suliban detainees attempt to escape the Tandaran Detention Complex 26, but were killed before they could succeed after Colonel Grat claimed they were all armed.
Travis Mayweather's father sends a letter to Archer strongly recommending his son for the helmsman position on Enterprise.
Archer's Comet is discovered by the crew of the Enterprise NX-01 and named after the ship's captain, Jonathan Archer.
Captain Jonathan Archer is instrumental in the liberation of Detention Complex 26 on Tandar Prime which later gives him a reputation of legendary proportions as an invincible warrior.
Duvall is promoted to Captain and is given command of the Shenandoah.
Earth reestablishes contact with the lost Terra Nova colony.

Earth Starfleet launches Enterprise NX-01, its first starship to use the warp five engine, opening up a new age of exploration for Earth. Enterprise also begins Humanity's involvement in the Temporal Cold War with the first encounter with the Cabal.
Enterprise has an encounter with the Ferengi, though the name of the species is not discovered until 2364.
Enterprise NX-01 arrives on a planet to scavenge material from a derelict Kantare supply ship. They offer the castaways repairs and ultimately rescue Ezral and Liana.
Enterprise NX-01 makes contact with Sluggo, whom they relocate to another planet after it has difficulty adjusting to the environment on the ship.
Enterprise NX-01 ran along a protostar a week before the incident at P'Jem.
Hernandez begins to move away from an explorer's mindset.
Humanity unofficially makes first contact with the Klingon Empire when Klaang crash-lands in Broken Bow, Oklahoma.
Earth also makes first contact this year with the Akaali, Axanar, Coridanites, Eska, Kantare, Malurians, Menk, Valakians and Xyrillians.
sup – Humanity makes first contact with the Andorians; the Andorian Imperial Guard finally corroborates the existence of a Vulcan surveillance listening post on P'Jem.
T'Pol confides in Commander Charles Tucker III that she had been arranged to be married, a situation that was causing her some concern.
The Andorians destroy the facility on P'Jem, though they give sufficient warning for personnel present to evacuate first.
The crew of Enterprise prevent the crew of the ECS Fortunate initiating hostilities between the Nausicaans and Earth Cargo Service freighters.
The Paraagan colony on Paraagan II was visited by outsiders. The foreman of the colony would later inform T'Pol of this fact during the Enterprise NX-01's visit to the planet six months later.
Travis Mayweather and Gannet meet each for the last time before the Coalition of Planets talks on Earth in 2155.
Trip Tucker experiences what is believed to be the first inter-species pregnancy involving a Human.
After the damage inflicted by the Romulan minefield, Enterprise visits a mysterious automated repair station. During the repairs, Travis Mayweather is abducted.

Captain Archer and Commander Tucker visit Keto-Enol and initiate a successful first contact with the Enolians. On their way out, they are arrested for possessing what the Enolian Guard believes to be contraband. As Archer and Tucker travel to Canamar on an Enolian transport, Kuroda hijacks the ship. With the help of an official, Enterprise finds the transport and rescues the crew.

Enterprise answers a distress call from a Retellian freighter. Tucker goes to help with the repairs but discovers the abduction of Kaitaama, the First Monarch of Krios Prime. The two end up on a planet until rescued by the crew of Enterprise. Eventually (September 12), a Krios Battlecruiser transports Kaitaama back to her planet.

Enterprise approaches a trinary star system housing a class IV black hole for observations. The radiation affects the behavior of the crew, except for T'Pol. When the rest of the crew is unconscious, T'Pol revives Archer and together they pilot the ship outside the stellar influence. However, Reed's earlier obsession with the ship's security results in a system that becomes standard procedure on the ship: tactical alert.

Enterprise arrives at Risa and some of her crew members take shore leave. Archer is approached by an attractive Tandaran agent, Keyla; Tucker and Reed are robbed; Mayweather breaks his leg, necessitating waking Phlox from his hibernation; Sato flirts with an alien man.

Enterprise finds a small craft from the future adrift in space, and both the Suliban and the mysterious Tholians are intent on retrieving it. First contact between an Earth vessel and the Tholian Assembly occurs.

Enterprise is apprehended by the Wisp ship. The Wisps attempt to take over her crew to continue their existence. Dr. Phlox, advised by T'Pol, floods the ship with CO2 and frees all the crew of the Wisps' influence. Enterprise quickly pulls away from their ship and destroys it by firing two spatial torpedoes into the interior.

Enterprise makes first contact with the Kreetassans, which ends in a misunderstanding. During their departure, a symbiotic lifeform attaches to Enterprise. It captures and assimilates four of the crew (Archer, Tucker, Rostov, and Kelly) but eventually it is transported to its homeworld.

Enterprise picks up a distress call from an alien craft. After rescue and repairs, Zobral invites Archer and Tucker for a meal in the desert of Cygniai Expanse. In the process, he requests their assistance with the war against the Torothans. In the process, the Torothans attack the encampment. Archer and Tucker are lost in the desert before being rescued by a shuttlepod.

Enterprise visits a pre-warp planet. Reed loses his communicator, which causes him and Archer to be arrested as spies of "The Alliance". To avoid cultural contamination, they play along until a cloaked Suliban cell ship, piloted by Tucker, rescues them.

Enterprise visits Kreetassa in a failed attempt to trade plasma injectors only to end in another misunderstanding after Porthos "waters" their Alvera trees. Archer eventually proceeds with a long and ritualistic apology, which is accepted.

Enterprise visits Tessik Prime. Crewman Fisher comes down with a case of Rigelian fever, necessitating the inoculation of the entire crew by Doctor Phlox with a vaccine containing millions of genetically-altered microbes.
In need of deuterium, the crew of Enterprise instructs alien mining colonists in how to defend themselves against Klingon harassers.

Interspecies Medical Exchange on Dekendi III. Feezal Phlox comes on board Enterprise and reunites with her husband; she helps Tucker install a neutron microscope. T'Pol's Pa'nar Syndrome is revealed and Archer tries to secure her position on the ship.

Jonathan Archer is convicted in a Klingon tribunal of assisting Arin'Sen renegades fleeing from Raatooras. He is incarcerated on Rura Penthe but flees. Relationships between the Klingon Empire and Earth continue to deteriorate.
October-December

On its way to Risa, Enterprise is ordered to retrieve Vulcan Ambassador V'Lar, who stands accused of criminal misconduct, from the planet Mazar. Enterprise becomes the first Earth starship to reach the speed of warp 5 while fleeing from Mazarite vessels.
On Pernaia Prime, Archer helps T'Pol apprehend Menos, a Vulcan fugitive.
Other Earth-alien first contacts this year include the Arkonians, the Kriosians, the Kreetassans, the Retellians, the Risians, Wisps, and the Takret.

sup - The crew of Enterprise attempts to visit an unexplored planet when a group of Takret fugitives warns them about a perilous neutronic wave front. The crew retires to the safety of the ship's reinforced catwalk while members of the Takret Militia board the ship looking for the fugitives.
T'Pau begins searching for the Kir'Shara.

The first known contact between vessels representing Earth and the Romulan Star Empire occurs after Enterprise encounters an outer-space minefield laid by the latter. Visual contact between crews is not made.
The mining colony on Paraagan II is destroyed by the Cabal in an effort to frame the crew of Enterprise and discredit their mission.

While testing a prototype auto-pilot, Tucker's shuttle is downed by an Arkonian pilot on one of a gas giant's 62 moons. Both men try to fix their respective ships but are stalled by the alien's distrust and hostile nature. Enterprise’s search efforts are further hampered by the selenium isotope on the moons' atmospheres, but eventually Tucker manages to use the alien's transceiver to contact the ship.

With the help of Archer as a mediator between the Andorians and Vulcans, Ambassador Soval and Commander Shran agree on a cease fire between the Vulcans and Andorians on Paan Mokar. Enterprise, under Tucker's command, stops an orbital conflict between the two forces. Shran and Soval end their negotiations in a positive mood, setting the stage for future negotiations.

An alternate timeline Enterprise commanded by Lorian attempts to stop the Xindi weapon from attacking Earth in the Delphic Expanse. The weapons are ineffective against the probe and Lorian has no choice but to set a collision course. His emotions take over and by the time he realizes his mistake, the probe had gone.
Archer and T'Pol study the Robinson Nebula, as they name it, composed of dark matter.

Earth is attacked by the Xindi probe from the Delphic Expanse. The attack decimates a large area between Florida and Venezuela, killing seven million people. Enterprise is recalled to Earth by Starfleet Command.
Enterprise destroys Duras' Bird-of-Prey during his final attempt to capture Archer seven weeks after leaving Earth and begins its search for the Xindi weapon in the Delphic Expanse.
Enterprise discovers the descendants of abducted Humans on a former Skagaran colony in the Delphic Expanse.
Enterprise enters the Orbital Drydock Facility for repairs and retrofits.

Enterprise makes a detour to gather scientific data on a planet that recently shifted orbit to relocate between two gas giants and where there is a lot of volcanic activity to be expected. Travis Mayweather asks for a leave to visit ECS Horizon. The cargo ship is attacked by pirates.

Enterprise reaches Earth. The IKS Bortas's, former captain: Duras, is given command of a Bird of Prey by the high council so he can regain his honor by capturing Jonathan Archer, Duras' Bird-of-Prey attacks Enterprise at the edge of the sol system and is chased off by three Starfleet ships.
In an alternate timeline, Enterprise captain Jonathan Archer's brain is infected by interspatial parasites, causing him to contract anterograde amnesia.
Kaitaama is set to become First Monarch on Krios Prime.
Earth also meets the Antarans, Osaarians, Sphere-Builders and Vissians this year.
Romulan agents kidnap the Aenar Gareb from Andoria Prime in order to use him as the pilot for their drone ship.
T'Pol secretly begins to inject herself with Trellium.
The construction of the second NX starship, Columbia, begins.

The Enterprise explores an uninhabited planet on which Phlox and T'Pol are contaminated. Archer is captured by Skalaar who intends to give him back to the Klingons. However in the process he helps him escape and return to the Enterprise.
The Enterprise makes first contact with the Vissians when they both study a hypergiant. Relations are tainted by Trip Tucker's interest in one cogenitor in the Vissian starship.

The Enterprise "rescues" three Denobulan scientists of the Denobulan Science Academy from the caves of Xantoras; meanwhile the Enterprise aids a ship which has problems with a reactor leak above the planet.
The war between the various Triannon factions comes to an end with the destruction of the surface of the planet Triannon.

The wreckage of a Borg sphere is discovered at the Arctic Circle on Earth having been buried there for 90 years. Two Borg drones are revived and manage to acquire a transport and escape Earth before they are intercepted. Enterprise catches up with them and manages to destroy the Borg-assimilated transport, but the Borg manage to send a subspace message to the Delta Quadrant that will take 200 years to arrive.
The Xindi-Reptilian scientist Damron travels to Earth in the year 2004 in an attempt to wipe out the Humans, though he is foiled by the intervention of Daniels.
Udar leaves the Augment camp on Trialas IV.
Acting on covert Romulan advice, Administrator V'Las bombs the United Earth Embassy on Vulcan to discredit its staff.
After the intervention of Enterprise in 1944 of an alternate timeline, the Temporal Cold War ostensibly comes to an end.
Arik Soong gives up his work in genetics and begins research in cybernetics.

As the crew of Enterprise repairs the ship, Archer is delivered by the Xindi-Aquatics in an escape pod. After he is recovered, Hoshi Sato discovers a hidden message by Degra in the pod, inviting him to a meeting in three days. Unable to arrive in time, Archer decides to pirate an Illyrian starship to steal its primary warp coil.
Columbia NX-02 is launched from drydock.
Corporal F. Hawkins is killed while defending T'Pol and Lieutenant Reed inside a Delphic Expanse sphere.
Degra dies after being stabbed by Dolim in his quarters on February 12th.

En route to the red giant Azati Prime, Enterprise NX-01 discovers a derelict Xindi-Insectoid starship on the surface of an uninhabited planet and apprehends a Xindi-Insectoid assault shuttle. The Enterprise crew discovers a hatchery, containing Xindi-Insectoid eggs, aboard the Insectoid ship; one of multiple tendrils attached to one of the eggs squirts Enterprise Captain Jonathan Archer with a mild neurotoxin, which makes him obsessive about their protection. He authorizes MACOs to be put in place, until T'Pol, Commander Charles Tucker III and Lieutenant Malcolm Reed lead a successful mutiny against Archer. Once he is cleared of the neurotoxin, though, he resumes command.

Enterprise arrives at Azati Prime and, thanks to the Xindi-Insectoid assault shuttle, the Enterprise crew manages to inspect the installation. Jonathan Archer decides to sabotage the Xindi weapon but is apprehended. In the process of his imprisonment, he gains Degra's trust. Enterprise is heavily damaged in the Battle of Azati Prime, where approximately eighteen crewmembers are killed. However, the attack is recalled by the Xindi-Primates.
Enterprise doctor Phlox is kidnapped and forced to help the Klingons discover a cure for a virus spreading through the Empire.
Enterprise finally returns to Earth after the conclusion of the Xindi incident.
Enterprise travels to the Barrens. There, the inventor of the transporter, Emory Erickson, tries to search for his lost son, Quinn, who is recovered but dies moments later.
In an alternate timeline, Earth is destroyed by the Xindi, who then proceed to wipe out most of the rest of Humanity.
Major J. Hayes is killed on February 13th of this year.
Notable Earth first contacts this year include the Illyrians, the Na'kuhl, and the Orions.

Preparing to use a subspace corridor to travel to the Xindi Council planet, Enterprise encounters a version of itself from an alternate timeline generated by the subspace corridor, a duplicate under the command of Lorian, who is the offspring of Tucker and T'Pol. The crew of the two versions of the ship disagree on the proper way to pass through a nebula controlled by a hostile species called the Kovaalans. T'Pol is unable to achieve more than an eighty-six percent reduction of the particle wake with impulse manifold modifications, but an elder version of herself shows her where to place an isomagnetic collector (a piece of Ikaaran technology) to reduce any residual particles. Lorian's Enterprise helps Archer's Enterprise to pass the corridor but doesn't emerge from the other side itself. Under Archer's command, Enterprise meets with Degra.
Relations between Earth and the Klingon Empire nearly boil over into war because of intervention by Arik Soong and a group of Augments who regard him as their father.
T'Pol offically joins Starfleet and is commissioned with the rank of Commander.

The crew holds a memorial for their dead shipmates. Enterprise arrives at a Delphic Expanse sphere, where it meets with Degra's ship. Archer briefs Degra about what he knows regarding the Delphic Expanse and the Sphere-Builders who are planning an invasion. Degra and Jannar offer to stop the launch of the weapon if Archer can prove the Xindi have been manipulated. After Archer reveals to them knowledge about the Xindi bio-weapon and other discoveries, Degra invites Archer to meet the Xindi Council to share this information. A Xindi-Reptilian warship arrives at the sphere, but is destroyed by Degra's ship, at Degra's instruction.

The Organians observe the crew of Enterprise reacting to an infection on board. Though Commander Tucker and Ensign Hoshi Sato die from the infection, Archer ultimately persuades the Organians to resurrect them. Owing to Organian influence, the Enterprise crew members retain no memories of their encounters with the species.

The Romulans continue attempts to destabilize Earth's region of space. A Romulan drone ship attacks Andorian and Tellarite travelers to a planned peace conference on Babel. Other ships attacked include a Rigelian scoutship. A Human, Vulcan, Andorian, and Tellarite alliance is formed to help the species work together to locate the drone ship and lead to talks on board Enterprise.

The Xindi weapon is destroyed by Jonathan Archer and his crew as it approaches Earth, thereby blighting the plan of the Sphere-Builders to annihilate Humanity and to prevent the Federation from forming in 2161.
Two unarmed Augments single-handedly hijack a Klingon Bird-of-Prey and kill its crew, setting in motion events that will significantly alter Klingon physiology for a period of time.
Another draft of the Geneva Protocol went into effect on Earth.
Charles Tucker III and T'Pol's "intimate" relationship ends.
Decontamination of Cold Station 12 is completed.
Hoshi Sato declares herself Empress of the Terran Empire after seizing control of the Defiant and ordering Starfleet's unconditional surrender.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: In the alternate timeline where Earth was destroyed by the Xindi, Enterprise NX-01 begins leading a fugitive convoy to the Ceti Alpha system.
Talks about the formation of a Coalition of Planets take place on Earth. Participating representatives include Humans, Vulcans, Andorians, Tellarites, Rigelians, Denobulans, and Coridanites.
The ISS Enterprise travels into Tholian space in search of a Terran vessel – the Constitution USS Defiant. The Enterprise is destroyed and most of the senior staff retrieve the Defiant.

The Terra Prime movement, under the leadership of John Frederick Paxton, attempts to expel all non-Humans from the Sol system by commandeering the verteron array on Mars. Their plan is foiled by the crew of the starship Enterprise.
Thy'lek Shran's second antennae is set to grow back.
In an alternate timeline in which the Xindi destroyed Earth, Enterprise NX-01 and its rag-tag fugitive fleet reach Ceti Alpha V.
In an alternate timeline in which the Xindi destroyed Earth, T'Pol resigns her commission and Charles Tucker III is promoted to Captain and assumes command of the Enterprise.
Talla, the daughter of Shran, an Andorian, and Jhamel, an Aenar, is born. Shran leaves the Andorian Imperial Guard around this time.
The Earth-Romulan War begins.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: The Redjac entity, incarnated as "Jack the Ripper", kills ten women in Heliopolis on Alpha Eridani II.
Janice Lee Raymond dies.
The Illyrian ship disabled by Enterprise NX-01 in 2154 was slated to have returned to the Illyrian star system in this year.
Shran fakes his own death in order to protect his family from criminals, business partners who believe he stole a Tenebian amethyst from them.
A starship disappears near the Taurean region.
The aborted film Star Trek: The Beginning was to be set during August and September of this year.
The leaders of two opposing factions on Kiley 279 meet in the same room. It will be the last time for a century.
The storyline for Star Trek: Legacy begins on 9 May of this year.
The Earth survey ship Velikan is launched.

The humiliating defeat of the Romulans at the Battle of Cheron effectively ends the Earth-Romulan War. This victory was achieved by an alliance comprised of Earth, Vulcan, Andorian, and Tellarite forces.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Charles Tucker III dies.
Enterprise NX-01 is retired from service.
Starfleet Academy is established.
The MACOs are stood down and their personnel absorbed into Starfleet.
Earth, Vulcan, Andoria and Tellar sign the treaties founding the United Federation of Planets at San Francisco.
Bethany is set to be released from prison had she served her full sentence.

In this year or shortly before, a Federation scout contacts the civilizations of Ornara and Brekka in the Delos system. The ship reports that Ornara is on the brink of spaceflight. Shortly afterwards, a plague erupts on Ornara. The plague kills many until a cure, felicium, is discovered on Brekka.
Jonathan Archer is made an honorary member of the Andorian Imperial Guard by General Thy'lek Shran.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: On Ligon II, supersedence is challenged by the First One, the ruling female of her planet.
The USS Franklin goes missing under Balthazar Edison while surveying the Gagarin Radiation Belt.
The Federation starship Archon visits the planet Beta III. The ship is destroyed and its crew is absorbed into the planet's society by the powerful computer Landru.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: The Federation starship USS Essex is destroyed by an electromagnetic storm caused by noncorporeal beings in orbit of a class M moon of Mab-Bu VI.
Briam of Krios Prime is born.

The starship Horizon visits Sigma Iotia II, a planet that is home to the pre-warp Iotians. One of the starship's crew members accidentally leaves behind a book, Chicago Mobs of the Twenties. The Horizon is lost shortly after leaving the planet, but transmits a radio frequency distress call that is not received by Starfleet until almost a hundred years later.
Flint acquires a bottle of Saurian brandy from this year.
Jonathan Archer is named Federation ambassador to Andoria.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Starfleet introduces a laser weapon which will not be superseded for at least one hundred years.

A group of North American Indians leave Earth in order to preserve their cultural identity and to find a new home. One hundred and eighty years later, they eventually settle on Dorvan V near the Cardassian border.
A neutron star in a binary star system located in the Kavis Alpha sector explodes.
The Dax symbiont first serves as a field docent for a Trill initiate.

Human children may well be growing up on Earth colonies as far from Earth as Paraagan II by this date, according to Trip Tucker, who tentatively christens one of the hypothetical settlements "New Sausalito".
Tieran's rule as Autarch of Ilari ends with Tieran forced into exile.
Approximate year that Slug-o-Cola begins using its famous slogan, "The slimiest cola in the galaxy" according to Rom.
Work begins on the Krenim weapon ship sometime around this year. However, the weapon is never built.
A batch of single malt whisky is made. The Species 8472 Boothby replicant enjoyed a glass of whisky claimed to be from this year in 2375.
Jonathan Archer becomes a member of the Federation Council.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: A further estimated passage of the Nexus through the galaxy.
Jonathan Archer finishes an eight year tenure as Federation Councilman representing United Earth.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: The Earth colony ship Eagle Valley is launched.
Jonathan Archer is elected Federation President.
entities: President of the United Federation of Planets

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Carol Marcus later dates a century of Starfleet-kept peace from this year.

episodes.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

events.summary: A starship disappears near the Taurean region.
The Earth survey ship SS Hatteras is launched.
The Federation begins to sound the alarm about metal toxicity on Urna.
A distress beacon, first used by starships working for the European Hegemony in 2123, is discontinued.
Jonathan Archer steps down as President of the United Federation of Planets.
entities: President of the United Federation of Planets

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Robert April is born.
Starfleet retires the last Daedalus starship.
Philippa Georgiou is born on Earth.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: George Kirk born to Tiberius Kirk.
One of the Human males examined by Dr. McCoy in the Omicron colony was born in this year.
Transporter psychosis is first diagnosed on Delinia II.

Harry Kim's "uncle" Jack pilots a sleeper ship on a deep-space exploration trip to Beta Capricus. The rest of the crew go into stasis, while Jack spends the six months piloting the ship himself. Upon arrival, the "star" is found not to exist and to be only an electromagnetic echo of a distant galaxy. Jack chooses not to wake the rest of the crew before returning, after a further six-month journey, back to Earth.
Jack's sleeper ship returns to Earth from its mission to Beta Capricus and its puzzled crew is revived from stasis, asking why they had never left orbit.
A starship disappears near the Taurean region.
Another reckoned passage of the Nexus through the galaxy.
The star systems in the Selcundi Drema sector are mapped for the first time by Federation unmanned probes. Drema V explodes in this year.
USS Valiant makes first contact with Eminiar VII. Contact is lost with the Valiant when they become a casualty in the interplanetary war between Eminiar VII and Vendikar.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: According to a speech given by John Gill, the Zeon colony on Ekos was established shortly after this year. A half a century later, this colony is wiped out virtually overnight by a genocide purge ordered by Gill who was then under the influence of his deputy Melakon.
Richard Daystrom is born.
Philippa Georgiou begins attending Starfleet Academy on Earth.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Montgomery Scott born.
Pralor Automated Unit 3947 is first activated.
Joseph M'Benga is born in Kenya on Earth.

Relations between the United Federation of Planets and the Klingon Empire degenerate, giving rise to some seventy years of unremitting hostility as the two powers become embroiled in a tense cold war.

episodes.title: In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II

events.summary: Philippa Georgiou graduates from Starfleet Academy.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Lela Dax dies at the age of 114 and the Dax symbiont is joined with Tobin.
Michael Burnham born on Earth.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Leonard McCoy born on Earth.
Ash Tyler is born.
La'An Noonien-Singh is born on Alpha I.
Dax attempts to solve an Altonian brain teaser for the first time.
Spock born at ShiKahr on Vulcan to Sarek and Amanda Grayson.

episodes.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier

events.summary: T'Pring is born on Vulcan.
Finnegan is born.
The Narada, displaced from 2387, destroys the USS Kelvin; Captain Robau dies aboard the Romulan ship.
Erica Ortegas born in Barranquilla, Colombia.
James T. Kirk born in Riverside, Iowa, to George and Winona Kirk.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home

events.timelineNote: Wikipedia marks its second 2233 block as alternate-timeline, covering Kirk's birth aboard the Kelvin. This Memory Alpha record is the prime-timeline birth in Riverside, Iowa, cited by TOS, ENT, SNW and Star Trek IV. Overlay match was a false positive.

events.summary: Sylvia Tilly born.
The survey ship USS Kelvin patrols 75,000 kilometres off the Federation–Klingon border.
A Zigwel Orgon develops the AP 350.
Una Chin-Riley enters Starfleet, and will not speak to Neera Ketoul again for twenty-five years.
A young Spock plays a prank, and is taunted by other Vulcan children over his human parentage.
A Klingon raid on Doctari Alpha orphans Michael Burnham and destroys the experimental Red Angel suit.
Alpha Lupi goes supernova.
Amanda Grayson takes Michael Burnham to a book exchange on a moon of Eridani D for her tenth birthday.
Burnham flees Sarek's home into Vulcan's Forge; her mother, reaching back through time, guides Spock to her.
May Ahearn born.
Logic extremists bomb the Vulcan Learning Center; Sarek revives Michael Burnham using his katra.
The Anti-Food Replication Movement is active this year.
The SS Columbia crashes on Talos IV; Vina alone survives the expedition.
Spock earns a reputation as a prankster in his home city of ShiKahr.
In an alternate timeline Spock dies as a child, Amanda leaves Sarek, and she later dies at Lunaport.
In a further variant timeline, both Spock and his sehlat I-Chaya survive.
Nyota Uhura born in Kenya.
Spock undergoes the kahs-wan survival ordeal on Vulcan.
T'Pring and Spock are bonded as children through a mind meld.
The Ba'ul of Kaminar achieve warp capability.
I-Chaya, the sehlat Sarek gave Spock, dies defending the boy from a le-matya.
The USS Enterprise passes briefly through this year while slingshotting back to 2267.
Robert Johnson born.
Brack buys the planet Holberg 917G.
The USS Archimedes makes first contact with the Ba'ul, answering a signal sent by the Kelpien Saru.
Elaine Johnson born.
Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po born.
entities: Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po

events.summary: A further starship vanishes near the Taurean system.
Montgomery Scott enters Starfleet, beginning fifty-two years of engineering service across eleven ships.
Pavel Chekov born, four years ahead of his prime-timeline counterpart.
Vader, of the Caldos colony, dies on 23 September.
Arlene Galway born.
Richard Daystrom invents duotronic computing, winning the Nobel and Zee-Magnees Prizes.
The USS Farragut is launched.
Birth of one of the Omicron colonists later examined by Leonard McCoy.
Straal and Paul Stamets begin their collaboration.
Emony Dax judges a gymnastics meet at the University of Mississippi and steers a young Leonard McCoy toward medicine.
In an alternate timeline, the Enterprise launches from the Luna Shipyards.
Michael Burnham becomes the first human admitted to the Vulcan Science Academy.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Pavel Chekov born in Russia to Andrei Chekov.
Federation and Klingon forces fight to a draw at Donatu V, near Sherman's Planet.
The USS Cayuga is launched.
The USS Constellation launches under Captain Matthew Decker, beginning the first of three five-year missions.
The USS Enterprise launches from the San Francisco Fleet Yards under Captain Robert April, with Sarah April as chief medical officer and Christopher Pike as first officer.
An exotic fungus destroys most of the planet Tarsus IV's food supply.
Captain Robert April chooses to break the General Order 1 and warn the Perricans about a possible apocalyptic meteor shower that was due to hit their planet.

Doctor Tristan Adams begins to revolutionize and Humanize prisons and the treatment of prisoners by developing new methods to treat the criminally insane. Meanwhile, the Federation begins to convert a number of their penal colonies over to asylums to treat inmates, rather than to "cage" them up.

James T. Kirk – at the age of thirteen – is one of nine surviving eyewitnesses to a massacre of some four thousand colonists on Tarsus IV by Governor Kodos. After supply ships arrive, Earth Forces find a burned body that is assumed to be Kodos, the case is closed. Still alive, Kodos secretly assumes the identity of actor Anton Karidian.
As he claims, this is the last time before 2267, that Montgomery Scott sees a PXK pergium reactor.
Dusty Swender is born in Syracuse, New York, North America.
Lenore Karidian is born.
Muliq Al Alcazar is born in the Tendara Colony.
Oriana is born.
Sylvia Tilly graduates from Musk Junior High School.
T'quiel Dawn is born in North Continent, Cerberus Colony.

The first of many thousands of lives lost in the Eminiar-Vendikar War in a star system of NGC 321 is recorded by the United Federation of Planets. After suffering for nearly two decades this mounting loss of life, the Federation would seek permission from the planet Eminiar VII for the construction of a treaty port in their star system in 2267.
entities: Unnamed Alpha and Beta Quadrant star systems

events.summary: Andrea Lopez is born in Starbase 2.
Captain April recommends that Una Chin-Riley receive the Starfleet Medal of Gallantry for her actions during the Marcel disaster.
Captain Robert April chooses to break the General Order 1 by sharing Federation technology with the inhabitants of Nar'el, so they could avoid an extinction-level drought.
Maat Al-Salah is born on Outpost 4.
Rukiya is born.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: Starfleet loses contact with the probe Friendship 1, launched in 2067.
Yuuto Hoshide is born in Misawa, Aomori, Japan.
As a lieutenant, Christopher Pike travels to Majalan system and meets Alora when he rescues her from a shuttle accident.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: Charles Evans is born.
Joanna McCoy, daughter of Leonard McCoy, is born.
Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po builds a universal translator.
entities: Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po

events.summary: Michael Burnham leaves the Vulcan Science Academy for a seven-year posting aboard the USS Shenzhou under Philippa Georgiou.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Spock refuses a Vulcan Science Academy place after a minister calls his human mother a disadvantage, and enlists in Starfleet.
Sylvia Tilly, at the age of sixteen, went through a "rebellious phase" in her life, and she was involved with hacking.
Tina Lawton is born.
After serving as first officer under Captain April, Christopher Pike receives a promotion to captain and assumes command of the Enterprise.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his first five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
James T. Kirk is introduced to Gary Mitchell. Years later, they became friends when cadet Mitchell attended Lieutenant Kirk's philosophy class at Starfleet Academy.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: Lucille Harewood born on Earth.
Robert April leaves the USS Enterprise as its commander for an appointment as Federation ambassador-at-large.
Sarek becomes estranged from his son for the next eighteen years.
Spock leaves Starfleet Academy as an ensign, having chosen it over the Vulcan Science Academy against Sarek's wishes.
Una Chin-Riley is made First officer.
Leland conducts a mission to Deneva that unfortunately involves the wrong ambassador.

Leonard McCoy heads a mass-inoculation program on the planet Dramia II. After his force leaves, a massive plague strikes the planet, killing most of its people. The Dramens hold him responsible and nineteen years later he is arrested, with Federation approval, and put on trial for the outbreak.
Charles Evans is the sole survivor of his ship's crash landing on the planet Thasus.

James T. Kirk begins a five-year officer training program at Starfleet Academy. In his first year at the Academy, he develops a romantic relationship with Ruth and is tormented by an older cadet named Finnegan.
Kirk befriends Lieutenant Commander Benjamin Finney, an instructor at Starfleet Academy.
May Ahearn dies.
A hypergiant observed one hundred years previously is expected to go supernova.
A new variant of the Starfleet uniform is introduced.
Doctor Leonard McCoy develops a medical procedure involving brain tissue engrafting.
Spock joins the USS Enterprise as science officer.
Commander Finney is reprimanded for the incident aboard the Republic.
Commander Hansen Al-Salah begins requesting new supplies for the Earth Outpost Stations along the Romulan Neutral Zone.
Ensign James T. Kirk is assigned with Lieutenant Commander Benjamin Finney to the USS Republic.
Leonard McCoy becomes romantically involved with the future Nancy Crater.
Spock begins programming the Kobayashi Maru scenario.
Spock is promoted to Lieutenant.
The Enterprise, en route to Vega colony to hospitalize the Rigel VII victims, is diverted by an eighteen-year old distress call from the SS Columbia, crashed on Talos IV.

The Enterprise under the command of Christopher Pike, visits Rigel VII. While commanding the landing party, Pike enters an abandoned fortress and fights a Kalar warrior. The landing party's ambush leads to seven injuries and three deaths, including Pike's yeoman Zac Nguyen.
The Terran Empire rebellion establishes a base on Harlak.

While aboard the training vessel Republic during a change of watches, Ensign Kirk relieves Commander Finney and notes that a circuit, which should have been closed, to the atomic matter piles is opened. After closing the circuit, Kirk logs the incident.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his second five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
Gabriel Lorca and Michael Burnham begin their attempted coup d'état against Emperor Philippa Georgiou.
Kirk brawls with Hendorff and other cadets in a Riverside bar; Christopher Pike breaks it up and urges him to enlist.
James T. Kirk leaves Starfleet Academy. He is assigned to the phaser station aboard the USS Farragut.
Newly divorced, Leonard McCoy enlists and meets Kirk aboard the same transport.
On his first planetary survey mission, Lieutenant Kirk visits the planet Neural, where he meets Tyree.
Starfleet cadets including Nyota Uhura and Hendorff pass through Riverside.
The Sheliak attempt to communicate with the Federation, something they will not do again until 2366.
The Terran Empire's new flagship, the ISS Charon, is launched from the Imperial Shipyard at Epsilon Indi IV.
The USS Enterprise is under construction at the Riverside Shipyard in Iowa.

A failed test of the USS Glenn’s experimental spore drive using a captured Tardigrade causes the crew to be mutilated. A group of Klingons try to take advantage of the disabled ship but are all killed by the tardigrade on board.

After being diverted from transfer to the Tellun penal colony, Michael Burnham and three other prisoners stay aboard the USS Discovery until their shuttle is fixed. While on board, Burnham learns about what the ship and her captain, Gabriel Lorca, are doing in their fight against the Klingons. Burnham is sent on an away mission with Sylvia Tilly and Paul Stamets to investigate the USS Glenn.
After finding that using the spore drive causes "Ripper" extreme pain, Michael Burnham releases the tardigrade from the Discovery. Its role is taken by Lieutenant Stamets.

Ambassador Sarek departs on a diplomatic mission, a meeting with a supposed dissenting Klingon faction. however his ship is bombed by a Logic extremist, stranding him inside a nebula until he can be retrieved in an unauthorized rescue mission by Michael Burnham. Katrina Cornwell goes in Sarek's place to meet with the Klingons, but is captured by them when the overture turns out to be a trap.
Captain Lorca has the Glenn scuttled, but recovers the tardigrade "Ripper" still on board and uses it to dramatically improve the Discovery’s spore drive.

Captain Lorca is captured by Klingons while returning from a conference. During his captivity, he encounters Starfleet Lieutenant Ash Tyler and Harcourt Fenton Mudd. Lorca and Tyler escape, leaving Mudd behind for helping the Klingons and are beamed aboard the Discovery after it arrives to find Lorca.

Harry Mudd uses a time crystal to trap Discovery inside a time loop in a attempt to figure out the starship's secret and sell the ship to the Klingons. Paul Stamets is able to exist outside of the loop and figures out his plan. In the end, Mudd is handed over to his fiance Stella and her father.

episodes.title: Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad

events.summary: Leonard McCoy ends his relationship with (the future) Nancy Crater.
Michael Burnham is found guilty of mutiny, stripped of her rank, and sentenced to life imprisonment.
Michael Burnham joins the crew of the Discovery.
Starfleet begins conceptualizing the spore drive, installing it on two prototype vessels, the USS Glenn and the USS Discovery.
Starfleet and Klingon fleets clash at the binary stars; Georgiou and T'Kuvma both die and the Federation-Klingon War opens.
The Enterprise is ordered to sit out the Klingon War.
The Klingon reactionary leader T'Kuvma begins assembling like-minded Klingons into a nationalist, anti-Federation movement.

The Shenzhou is called to a binary star system to investigate damage done to an interstellar relay by T'Kuvma's faction. First Officer Michael Burnham attempts a mutiny against Captain Philippa Georgiou in the hopes of defusing the crisis.
The Torchbearer Voq attempts to continue T'Kuvma's ideology, but is largely sidelined by the Klingon Empire.
The USS Buran is ambushed with only Gabriel Lorca surviving — though the survivor is his mirror-universe counterpart.

The USS Gagarin is ambushed simultaneously while patrolling its sector along with the two other Starfleet vessels in the area; the USS Hoover and USS Muroc. The Hoover and Muroc are destroyed, closely followed by the Gagarin after its warp drive is disabled. The USS Discovery responds to the Gagarin’s distress call but is unable to stop the Klingons destroying the vessel with all hands.
The USS Shenzhou visits the Crepusculan homeworld and saves them from a drought.
Voq is overthrown by Kol of the House of Kor, who assumes leadership of T'Kuvma's Klingons. Voq is abandoned aboard the wreck of the USS Shenzhou but is rescued by L'Rell.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: With the aid of the spore drive, Captain Lorca leads the Discovery to victory, destroying multiple Klingon ships during their attack on Corvan II.

episodes.title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry

events.summary: Zefram Cochrane Warp Research Facility opens.
Montgomery Scott and Keenser are posted to the Federation outpost on Delta Vega.
Christopher Pike assumes command of the Discovery.

Commander Saru leads the Discovery on a mission that results in the end of the Federation-Klingon War, with many of the crew members being awarded for their actions, including Saru receiving the Starfleet Medal of Honor.
Following a number of multiple catastrophic systems failures, The Enterprise is towed to spacedock for repairs.
Lieutenant Commander Una Chin-Riley begins an investigation into the allegations against Spock.
Spock takes leave and admits himself to the psychiatric ward on Starbase 5, then escapes by shuttle amid accusations of murder.

The Discovery crew, still led by Saru, make their way to Vulcan to pick up the ship's new commanding officer, but stop along the way to respond to a distress call from Captain Christopher Pike aboard the USS Enterprise.
The Discovery makes first contact with the New Eden colony on the planet Terralysium.
The Shakespearian troupe Karidian Company of Players, sponsored by the Galactic Cultural Exchange Project, begins its tour of official installations.
The USS Discovery returns from the mirror universe nine months after they left the prime universe.

episodes.title: The War Without, The War Within

events.summary: Two hundred crewmembers of the Farragut are killed at Tycho IV by a dikironium cloud creature. One of the casualties is the ship's commanding officer, Captain Garrovick. The Farragut first officer commends Lieutenant Kirk for his actions at Tycho IV.
Alexander Marcus finds the SS Botany Bay and presses Khan Noonien Singh into Starfleet service as John Harrison.
Ash Tyler is offered command of Section 31.

Christopher Pike is promoted to admiral. James T. Kirk becomes captain of the USS Enterprise as Christopher Pike's relief. Ambassador Spock leads survivors of Vulcan in forming a New Vulcan colony with advice to his younger self to continue in Starfleet.
Christopher Pike resumes command of the Enterprise.
events.timelineNote: Cited only by DIS 'Such Sweet Sorrow', which is prime canon. The overlay's alternate-timeline claim came from an adjacent Wikipedia block and does not apply.

events.summary: Citing Starfleet Regulation 157, section 3, Lt. Spock recommends to Starfleet that USS Discovery, its personnel, and its spore drive should not be discussed under penalty of treason.
Doctor Joseph M'Benga's daughter Rukiya is diagnosed with cygnokemia.

En route, cadet James Kirk, who has stowed away with the help of Medical Officer Leonard "Bones" McCoy, overhears the mission briefing and deduces that the circumstances around Vulcan are similar to those surrounding his birth, when the USS Kelvin was destroyed by an unidentified, technologically-advanced ship. He reports these conclusions to Pike, who thusly prepares the Enterprise for action.
In the official records of Starfleet, the USS Discovery is listed as destroyed. The records do not mention the spore drive, Red Angel, or Control.

James T. Kirk takes the Kobayashi Maru test for the third time, but this time he manages to secretly install a subroutine which changes the conditions of the test, thereby allowing Kirk to save the Kobayashi Maru and beat the test, becoming the only cadet to ever beat the no-win scenario.
Katie Wesley is born to Bob Wesley.

episodes.title: One of Our Planets Is Missing

events.summary: Kirk and Spock board the Narada to confront Nero and stop him. Spock steals the Jellyfish and Kirk fights with several guards, Ayel and eventually Nero. The Jellyfish destroys the drilling platform and escapes the area, to draw the battle away from Earth and sets a collision course for the Narada. He and Kirk along with Pike are transported away at the last second by the Enterprise, but the Jellyfish crashes into the Narada thereby igniting the red matter and causing a massive black hole, which consumes the Narada, and very nearly the Enterprise as well. This beam-out marks the first time Scott ever beams three people from two targets onto one pad.

Kirk, Olson and Sulu space-dive onto the platform. Olson, who had the charges, is killed in the descent; however, Kirk and Sulu succeed at disabling the drill. Unfortunately, the drill's work has already been completed, and Nero orders the red matter deployed. Kirk and Sulu beam back to the ship by the efforts of Ensign Pavel Chekov.

Nero is first seen by the Federation and tells the native Spock that he wants him to see something as well as order Captain Pike to come aboard the Narada, just like he requested Richard Robau to twenty-five years earlier.

On stardate 2258.42 (February 11), the planet Vulcan is destroyed along with, approximately, six billion Vulcans on-planet by Nero using red matter. The Vulcan species is rendered critically endangered with only an estimated ten thousand surviving. Captain Pike is taken hostage by aboard the Narada by Nero.
Pike agrees and is accompanied by Kirk, helmsman Hikaru Sulu, and chief engineer Olson. Pike appoints Spock acting captain and Kirk his first officer.

Spock and Kirk meet Montgomery Scott and Keenser at the Delta Vega outpost, and using his transwarp beaming equation, Spock transports Kirk and Scott to the Enterprise. After a fight which proves Spock to be emotionally compromised, Kirk assumes command and the pursuit of Nero. Meanwhile, Nero and the Narada head for Earth intent on destroying the planet. Chekov devises a plan to hide the Enterprise near Saturn to avoid the Narada’s detection and rescue Captain Pike.

Spock determines that the red matter will cause a black hole to develop inside Vulcan. While the planet evacuates, he beams down to reach several Vulcan dignitaries who reside in areas that are unreachable by transporter. He succeeds at saving some of them, including his father Sarek, but his mother Amanda Grayson is killed.

Spock, emotionally unbalanced by these traumas, orders a course for the Laurentian system to rendezvous with the rest of Starfleet in an effort to balance the odds for the next confrontation against Nero. Kirk objects and is marooned on Delta Vega. He gets chased by vicious animals and finds his rescuer is Spock himself, but 129 years older. Spock explains the situation to Kirk via a mind meld and they head for the Starfleet outpost fourteen kilometers away.

The Enterprise arrives only to find a debris field of the fleet, and the Narada hovering over Vulcan with a drilling platform in the lower atmosphere attempting to mine to the core. Nero orders the destruction of the Enterprise, only to realize what vessel he is firing on.

The Narada, a Romulan mining vessel from the future of the prime reality, destroys 47 Klingon warbirds near the Klingon prison planet. Later, it arrives at a location where the Jellyfish carrying Spock emerges from a black hole, created in the future to stop a star that had gone supernova and threatened the galaxy. Nero, captain of the Narada, captures the Jellyfish and maroons Spock on Delta Vega so he can observe the destruction of Vulcan.
The newly-repaired USS Enterprise departs from spacedock on a mission to Edrin II.
The USS Discovery passes through a wormhole to the year 3189.
Kirk takes the Enterprise out with the crew who helped him stop Nero.
Vulcan itself is destroyed. Spock privately estimates no more than 10,000 survivors, making him a member of an endangered species.
A new First Servant is selected on Majalis.

episodes.title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach

events.summary: A new variant of Starfleet uniform is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet communicator is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet flag officer uniform is introduced.
A new variant of the Starfleet tricorder is introduced.
A new variant of the Type 2 phaser is introduced.
After crash landing on Valeo Beta V, the crew is killed by Gorn hatchlings. The Peregrine is later salvaged by the Enterprise.
The Ketha Province has stood empty for decades by this point.
Harrison attacks a Starfleet summit, killing Pike, then escapes by transwarp beam to Qo'noS.
Kirk dies repairing the Enterprise's warp core as both ships fall toward Earth.
Cadet Nyota Uhura is assigned to the USS Enterprise as a communications officer.
Cadet Uhura is promoted to Ensign.
Captain Pike celebrates his birthday
Carol Marcus joins the Enterprise; Scott and Keenser resign over the sealed torpedoes, and Chekov takes engineering.
Christine Chapel is assigned to the USS Enterprise as a nurse.
Commander Chin-Riley is cleared of all charges against her and resumes her duties aboard the Enterprise.
Commander Pelia joins the Enterprise as chief engineer.
Doctor Joseph M'Benga is assigned to the USS Enterprise as chief medical officer.
Ensigns Brad Boimler and Beckett Mariner are unwittingly transported to this time briefly
Erica Ortegas is assigned to the USS Enterprise as a helmsman.
General Order 1 is renamed the "Prime Directive".
George Samuel Kirk is assigned to the USS Enterprise as a sciences division member.
Hemmer is assigned to the USS Enterprise as chief engineer.
In an alternate timeline, Captain James T. Kirk is the commanding officer of the Enterprise.
James T. Kirk is promoted to First officer.
Kirk breaches the Prime Directive to save Spock on Nibiru and is sent back to the Academy; Spock transfers to the USS Bradbury.
The Enterprise crew are caught up in the Mudd Incident and seize a K'normian trading ship.
Harrison forces Section 31 agent Thomas Harewood to bomb the Kelvin Memorial Archive in London.
La'An Noonien-Singh is assigned to the USS Enterprise as acting first officer and later chief of security.
Lieutenant Hemmer commits suicide on Valeo Beta V to prevent Gorn eggs from hatching.
Lieutenant La'An Noonien-Singh takes a leave of absence from Starfleet to help Oriana find her family.
Following Kirk's coordinates, Scott finds the hidden yard where the USS Vengeance is being built and stows away aboard her.
McCoy and Marcus open one of the long-range torpedoes and find a cryogenically preserved human inside.
Rukiya is cured of her illness and is transformed into a state of pure energy.
Spock and T'Pring get engaged.
The Enterprise investigates an abandoned Illyrian colony on Hetemit IX, stranding Pike and Spock planetside.
Stardate 1457.9. Lieutenant Commander Chin-Riley is taken into custody for violating the Starfleet code of Conduct.
Stardate 1630.1. The Enterprise returns to Rigel VII under suspicion of cultural contamination, discovering and arresting former yeoman Zac Nguyen in the process.
Stardate 2394.8. Captain Pike is promoted to Fleet captain.
Stardate 2394.8. Fleet captain Pike is temporarily given command of the Farragut.
Stardate 2912.4. The Enterprise makes first contact with the Shepherds.

Stardate 3177.3-3177.9. The first known contact between vessels representing the United Federation of Planets and the Gorn Hegemony occurs after Enterprise encounters several Gorn ships after they attack the Finibus III colony. Actual contact between crews is not made.
The Archer is tasked with making first contact with the Kiley.
The Enterprise departs Starbase 1.
The Enterprise makes official first contact with the Kiley.
The Farragut is stationed at Starbase 1.
The planet Cerberus is stricken by crop failure during Joanna McCoy's schooling there. Carter Winston uses his personal fortune to save the colony.
The R'ongovian Protectorate joins the United Federation of Planets.
The Stamets-type shuttlecraft is introduced.
The Enterprise's warp core fails past the Neutral Zone; Kirk's landing party is intercepted by Klingons while hunting Harrison.
The Vengeance overhauls the Enterprise near Luna; Marcus orders her destroyed, but Scott disables the attacker's weapons.
The year Kirk would have finished the Academy had he taken the usual four years.
Kirk recovers, and Khan is returned to cryogenic storage.
Una Chin-Riley is given command of the USS Archer.
Captain Matthew Decker finishes his third five-year mission aboard the Constellation.
Carol Marcus formally joins the Enterprise crew.
Repaired, the Enterprise sets out on a five-year exploratory mission.
A recovered Kirk speaks on Federation principles at a memorial ceremony.
The Cayuga is destroyed by the Gorn over Parnassus Beta.
The Enterprise's first day in deep space.
The Stardiver is destroyed by the Gorn in the Shangdi system.
Cyrano Jones, a licensed asteroid locator and prospector, begins selling and buying rare merchandise.
Dr. Roger Korby sends his last transmission from the planet of Exo III.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: James T. Kirk is romantically involved with Janet Wallace.
La´an Noonien-Singh is tasked with testing Starfleet's new technology recreation room, or holodeck to see if it can withstand genuine rigorous circumstances.
On 892-IV, Flavius Maximus wins his first arena game. For the next seven years, he was the most successful gladiator of his province.
Professor Robert Crater and his wife Nancy arrive on the planet M-113. The professor begins his archaeological excavation of an ancient and extinct civilization that once lived there.
Spock meets Leila Kalomi on Earth.
The United Federation of Planets celebrates 100 years since its founding
The USS Enterprise goes on an undercover mission the pre-warp planet Tezaar. Four of the five members of the away team have their dna temporarily altered to appear Vulcan to the sensors on Tezaar.
Areel Shaw and James T. Kirk meet each other for the last time before Kirk's trial on Starbase 11 in 2267.
R.M. Merik becomes the Lord of the Games on 892-IV.
SS Beagle crash-lands on 892-IV after having sustained heavy meteor damage. The ship's crew is captured by the inhabitants.
Kirk records a captain's log entry.
entities: Captain's log, USS Enterprise (NCC-1701 alternate reality)

events.summary: Kirk records a further captain's log entry.
entities: Captain's log, USS Enterprise (NCC-1701 alternate reality)

events.summary: Kirk's attempt to broker peace between the Fibonan Republic and the Teenaxi Delegation collapses.
Ambassador Spock dies on New Vulcan.
An infant Lactran, later encountered by the USS Enterprise landing party in 2269, is born on Lactra VII.
Ben Childress, Herm Gossett, and Benton partner up to establish a mining company on Rigel XII.
A new USS Enterprise begins construction at Starbase Yorktown.
Kirk marks his thirtieth birthday at a surprise party, toasting the Enterprise and its dead.
Jaylah is admitted to Starfleet Academy on Kirk's recommendation.
Krall's assault on Starbase Yorktown is broken by the Enterprise crew flying the salvaged Franklin.
Matthew Decker is promoted to commodore. He remains in command of the USS Constellation.
Jaylah leads Montgomery Scott to the intact USS Franklin on Altamid.

One hundred fifty men, women, and children, led by Elias Sandoval, depart Earth for Omicron Ceti III to establish the future Omicron colony. It is the last time they will encounter anyone outside their own group until the USS Enterprise's 2267 arrival.
Among Ambassador Spock's effects, Spock finds a photograph of the Enterprise senior crew from the other timeline.
Krall's swarm ships destroy the Enterprise over Altamid.
The Enterprise puts in at Yorktown Station to resupply.
Carter Winston disappears in a meteor storm. His spacecraft crashes on the planet Vendor, where he is saved by an inhabitant of the planet.
In an alternate timeline, Kirk commands the USS Enterprise.
In the same timeline, Commander Thelin becomes the first officer of the Enterprise.
Spock visits his parents on Vulcan for the last time in the next four years.
The Omicron colony is established at Omicron Ceti III.
The USS Reliant is launched.
Tuvok is born on Vulcanis Lunar colony.
A Dom Pérignon of this vintage is kept back to christen the Enterprise-B in 2293.
Starfleet introduces a new uniform, worn alongside the older style into the 2270s.
April Wade is born.

At a Christmas party held in the Enterprise science lab, Captain Kirk meets Dr. Helen Noel. She later recalls him "dropping in" on the party, during a mission to the rehabilitation colony on Tantalus V.
Captain Christopher Pike is again promoted to the rank of fleet captain.
Kirk takes command of the Enterprise for a five-year mission of exploration.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: Carter Winston dies on the planet Vendor.
Ingraham B is attacked by flying parasites.
Nancy Crater is killed by planet M-113's last surviving native.
The Borg overrun the El-Aurian homeworld, scattering survivors across the galaxy.

The Enterprise becomes one of the first Earth ships to reach the edge of the Milky Way Galaxy, where they encounter the galactic barrier. Gary Mitchell and Elizabeth Dehner are among the Enterprise officers who die in the line of duty.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: The Klingons and the Federation sign a peace treaty which they honor over the next three years.
A Romulan ship crosses into Federation space and destroys several Earth Outpost Stations, ending over a century of Romulan isolation. This incident comes to be known as the Neutral Zone Incursion.
A transport from Ingraham B to Deneva carries the deadly flying parasite that caused mass insanity on Ingraham B in 2265.
Leonard McCoy succeeds Mark Piper as the Enterprise's chief medical officer.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country

events.summary: Dr. McCoy spills acid in sickbay, an event which also apparently happens on the mirror universe ISS Enterprise at some point prior to 2267.
Dr. Simon Van Gelder is assigned to the Tantalus Penal Colony as associate director, serving on Tristan Adams' staff.
Due to a transporter malfunction, Captain James T. Kirk is temporarily split into a positive and a negative half.

Famed archeologist Roger Korby is found alive on the planet Exo III, where he discovered remains of ancient technology. However, it turns out Korby is actually an android duplicate of his former self. He perishes along with the other androids on the surface.

episodes.title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?

events.summary: In an alternative timeline, where Enterprise is captained by Captain Christopher Pike, this resulted in open warfare between Federation and Romulans.

Kodos the Executioner is discovered to still be alive, despite having been thought dead since 2246. While under surveillance by Kirk, Kodos is accidentally killed by his daughter Lenore before formal charges can be brought against him for crimes committed on Tarsus IV.
Lieutenant Hikaru Sulu transfers from staff physicist to helm officer aboard the USS Enterprise.

Lieutenant Nyota Uhura becomes chief communications officer and Janice Rand becomes the captain's yeoman on the USS Enterprise. Dave Bailey is promoted to lieutenant and chief navigator despite some concerns about his lack of experience.
On board the USS Enterprise, Dr. McCoy, in his quarters, kills the last M-113 creature as it tries to kill Kirk. The M-113 creature had already killed four Enterprise crewmen.

Six months before the Neutral Zone Incursion, an accident aboard a class J starship releases delta radiation, killing several Starfleet cadets and severely injuring Fleet Captain Christopher Pike, confining him to a wheelchair.
The Antares is destroyed, with all hands on board perishing, when Charles Evans with psionic powers makes a baffle plate on the shield of the ship's energy pile "go away".
The colonists of Janus VI create a detailed series of charts for the twenty-two levels of the mine. These charts later prove useful in the search for the Horta in 2267.
The inhabitants of Acamar III establish peace after generations of wars. An exception are the Gatherers, who continue to be violent for a century.
The planet Psi 2000 disintegrates naturally.
The Redjac entity, incarnated as "Beratis", kills several women on Rigel IV.
The UFP makes first contact with the First Federation after an encounter with Balok, commander of the flagship Fesarius. As an intercultural exchange, Dave Bailey transfers to serve on the vessel.
The USS Enterprise charts the L-370 system.
The USS Enterprise undergoes a refit both on the outside and the inside design sometime after 2265.

episodes.title: Where No Man Has Gone Before

events.summary: A transporter accident brings four USS Enterprise personnel from the primary universe to the mirror universe.
