A Borg vessel carrying Seven of Nine and several other Borg drones crashes on a planet on the Delta Quadrant. The drones are separated from the Borg Collective and start to regain their identities, but are relinked into a temporary collective established by Seven of Nine.

A peace treaty is established ending the centuries-long war between Krios Prime and Valt Minor. Despite Ambassador Briam's being incapacitated by injury, this treaty is made possible by the diplomatic assistance of Jean-Luc Picard and the dutiful self-sacrifice of the metamorph Kamala.

A probe from the long-dead planet Kataan telepathically transmits information about its culture, in the form of memories of a man named Kamin, into the brain of Captain Picard. Picard recovers a Ressikan flute from the probe, a relic from the entire lifetime he experienced while unconscious for merely twenty-five minutes.

Ambassador Spock is sighted on Romulus and the Federation Council worries that he has possibly defected. He is actually working with an underground movement that has the goal of unifying the Vulcan and Romulan peoples. Romulan officials, led by Sela, try to exploit the underground to send an invasion fleet to Vulcan, but Captain Picard, Lieutenant Commander Data, and Spock uncover the plot and the invasion forces are destroyed.
B'Elanna Torres drops out of Starfleet Academy.
Beckett Mariner enters Starfleet Academy as a first-year cadet.
Bok buys himself out of Rog Prison.
Captain Picard is able to open communication with the enigmatic Tamarians.
Commander Chakotay visits Starfleet Command for the last time before being marooned in the Delta Quadrant, and resigns his commission to Admiral Nimembeh.
Doctor April Wade is nominated for the Carrington Award.
Enabran Tain retires as head of the Obsidian Order and settles on the Arawath Colony.
Fesek's son is born on Malon Prime.
Gowron secludes himself and begins to rewrite Klingon history, excising any mention of the Enterprise’s help.
Hector Ilario enters Starfleet Academy.

In a second quantum reality, Worf and Troi began a romantic relationship while he was recovering from his spinal injury on Stardate 45587. Six months later, Worf formally asked Riker's permission to court Troi as he felt that not doing so would be dishonorable. The two eventually married.
In another quantum reality, Commander Worf, the first officer of the Enterprise-D, and his wife Deanna Troi had their second child, a girl named Shannara.
Julian Bashir, Elizabeth Lense, and Erit graduate from the Starfleet Medical Academy. Lense was valedictorian and Bashir was salutatorian.
Kira Nerys is fighting the Cardassians in a nameless swamp on Bajor.

episodes.title: In the Hands of the Prophets

events.summary: Lieutenant Aquiel Uhnari is assigned to Relay Station 47.

Lieutenant Commander Geordi La Forge develops a tachyon detection grid in order to detect cloaked ships and reveal the Romulan involvement. They are forced to withdraw, leaving the Duras without support and Gowron victorious.

Lieutenant Commander Geordi La Forge develops a tachyon detection grid in order to detect cloaked ships and reveal the Romulan involvement. They are forced to withdraw, leaving the Duras without support and Gowron victorious.
Mezoti is born.
Neelix visits a planetoid with rich dilithium signatures that will later be visited by the USS Voyager in 2371.

Nova Squadron, including Cadet Wesley Crusher, is involved in a deadly accident at the Saturn flight range. The members of the team were attempting a Kolvoord Starburst, a maneuver banned by Starfleet Academy because of its danger. The team covers up the accident, but evidence uncovered by Captain Picard convinces Wesley to come forward with the truth.
Richard Bashir tells his son he could do research on Earth instead of Deep Space 9.

Sometime this year, to cope with the stress of living aboard Bajoran space station Deep Space 9, Elim Garak activates the Obsidian Order cranial implant designed to help him resist torture and leaves it running continuously for the next two years.
Starfleet introduces the Danube runabout.
The celebrated ambassador Sarek of Vulcan dies at the age of 203 of complications from Bendii Syndrome.
The Enterprise attempts to communicate with the Crystalline Entity, but it is destroyed by Dr. Kila Marr.

The Enterprise is struck by a quantum filament and experiences heavy damage including a near-loss of antimatter containment. Molly O'Brien, daughter of Miles and Keiko O'Brien, is born in the ship's Ten Forward lounge during the crisis.
The first test of the soliton wave is conducted near Bilana III.

The Klingon Civil War between the Duras family and Gowron continues. Although Gowron's forces are outnumbered and on the verge of defeat, a Starfleet armada led by Jean-Luc Picard and the USS Enterprise blockades Romulan support of Duras.
The Ktarians attempt to take control of the Federation, but are thwarted by Enterprise-D personnel.
The Romulans experiment with interphase cloaking technology but fail to perfect it.
The SS Vico is destroyed in the black cluster. Its remains are towed back to Starbase 514 to be scrapped.

The USS Bozeman emerges from a temporal causality loop in which it was trapped for ninety years. In the process, it collides with the Enterprise-D and causes a temporal loop to form at the time of the collision. The loop repeats for seventeen days until the Enterprise crew discovers how to avoid the collision.
Tuvok receives his meditation lamp from a Vulcan master, paying twice the normal price.
Unknown at the time, Hugh's return causes widespread systems problems in the Borg, resulting in many drones simply being cut off from the Collective.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-second year in a row.
A back-up file is installed on the Cardassian ATR-4107 missile in this year, on stardate 46437.5.
A Cardassian sneak attack on the Federation system Minos Korva, staged in the McAllister C-5 Nebula, is prevented by Captain Edward Jellico, temporarily in command of the Enterprise-D.
A crucial victory is won for Spock's reunification efforts on Romulus when Vice-Proconsul M'ret defects to the Federation.

A duplicate of William T. Riker created due to a transporter accident, known as Thomas Riker, is rescued from the outpost on Nervala IV, where he was stranded for eight years. Thomas Riker is later transferred to the USS Gandhi.
A Jem'Hadar ship crashes on Bopak III. The only survivor is Goran'Agar, who unexpectedly survives for thirty-five days without ketracel-white.
Starfleet issues another uniform variant, black but for division colour at the shoulders.
Arjin's father dies.
Benjamin Sisko meets General Krim for the first time, during a session of the Bajoran Executive Committee.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is taken captive by Cardassian forces on Celtris III and tortured by Gul Madred. He is released after the embarrassing defeat of the Cardassians at the McAllister Nebula.
Captain Silva La Forge becomes the commanding officer of the USS Hera.
Data paints a pair of two oil paintings on canvas, one of which he titles Daughter, and gifts them to Jean-Luc Picard while onboard the Enterprise-D.
Ensign Ro Laren departs the Enterprise-D to participate in Starfleet's Advanced Tactical Training program.
Ferengi Pirate DaiMon Lurin hijacks the USS Enterprise-D.

Kai Opaka is left in the Gamma Quadrant on a moon occupied by two warring factions. Unable to leave the moon ever again, she hopes to ultimately end the fighting. Also, The United Federation of Planets is comprised of over one hundred planets as of this year.
Keiko O'Brien opens a school on Deep Space 9.
Kes is born on Ocampa.
Montgomery Scott is discovered to be alive, suspended in the transporter buffer of the USS Jenolan. The USS Enterprise discovers a Dyson sphere upon locating the Jenolan.
Neelix hangs a preserved Talaxian Furfly in the engine room of the Baxial.

On Deep Space 9, Chief Miles O'Brien unintentionally activates a long-dormant aphasia device while repairing the command level replicators. The device was planted there by Bajorans Dekon Elig and Surmak Ren when the Cardassians were in command of the station in 2351. An aphasia virus sweeps through DS9, threatening to eliminate the station's personnel. Fortunately, Major Kira Nerys is able to locate Dr. Surmak and he discovers an antidote.

On Earth, the quadricentennial of Apollo 11’s landing on the moon in 1969 is celebrated. Starfleet Academy gives out a certificate in relation to it, in some way. One of the recipients is Cadet Harry Kim.
Seska, an agent of the Obsidian Order, joins the Maquis, posing as a Bajoran.

Several weeks later, the Enterprise-D returned to Deep Space 9 to help repair the Bajoran aqueduct systems that had been damaged during the Occupation. Worf learns that survivors of the Khitomer Massacre, including his father, may have been captured and imprisoned by the Romulans in the Carraya system by the Yridian information dealer Jaglom Shrek. He discovers the camp, and while his father is not among the survivors, there are indeed captured Klingons, now living in peace with their Romulan captors. Several younger members of the camp decide to leave with Worf and are sworn to secrecy about the existence of the camp in order to preserve the honor of families back in the Klingon Empire. Meanwhile on the Enterprise, Data experiences a serious of visions due to an accident while helping DS9 Chief Medical Officer Julian Bashir. Data discovers that these visions are dreams. The dreams are those of his father: the late Doctor Noonien Soong.

Shortly afterwards, Commander Benjamin Sisko and Lieutenant Jadzia Dax discover the Bajoran wormhole, the first stable wormhole known to exist. Sisko makes direct contact with the Prophets, becoming the Emissary of the Prophets as prophesied in ancient Bajoran mythology.
The Cardassian Union ends its occupation of the planet Bajor, which lasted fifty years. As a result, the Cardassian Velos VII internment camp is closed on stardate 46302.

The clerics on Boreth create a clone of Kahless the Unforgettable, hoping to use him to reunite the Klingon people. Worf later uncovers the fact that this Kahless is a clone, and the clone is installed as a ceremonial leader of the Klingon people.

The crew of the Enterprise-D, along with Romulan, Klingon, and Cardassian interests, deciphers the last work of Professor Galen, revealing that a race of ancient humanoids is a common ancestor of most species in the Milky Way Galaxy.

The mining station Terok Nor in orbit of Bajor is abandoned during the Cardassian retreat. The Bajoran Provisional Government requests Starfleet assistance in maintaining the station, and it is renamed Deep Space 9. The Enterprise-D arrives to offload personnel and equipment.
The teachers on the ship agree not to hold Captain Picard Day in 2370, according to Jean-Luc Picard, a promise they will break.
The Vulcan Isolationist Movement hires mercenaries to find the remaining two pieces of the Stone of Gol.
The Vulcan Isolationist Movement steals a piece of the Stone of Gol from a Vulcan museum.
Toman'torax becomes Omet'iklan's Second.
Vash arrives at Deep Space 9 after two years in the Gamma Quadrant in the company of Q.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-third year in a row.
Federation colonists who reject the Cardassian treaty take up arms as the Maquis.

A second Borg incursion into Federation space continues (having begun in 2369, around stardate 46982.1), as individualized Borg drones are led by Lore, a Soong-type android. However, their attempt to annihilate the Federation is once again foiled by Captain Jean-Luc Picard and the USS Enterprise. Lore is defeated, deactivated, and dismantled.

Aboard the USS Prometheus, Gideon Seyetik makes an attempt to reignite Epsilon 119. To free his wife, Nidell, from their marriage, he pilots a shuttlecraft into the star, killing himself, but bringing Epsilon 119 back to life.
After Elim Garak becomes ill after overuse of a cranial implant, Julian Bashir receives help from Enabran Tain to save his friend's life.
Although the rescue of the Siskos, Nog, and Quark is successful, the Galaxy starship USS Odyssey is destroyed by a Jem'Hadar suicide attack.
Alyssa Ogawa marries Andrew Powell. The couple also conceived a child.
Alyssa Ogawa, Sam Lavelle, and Taurik are promoted to lieutenant junior grade.
An emergent lifeform uses the systems on board the USS Enterprise-D to reproduce, temporarily giving the Enterprise sentience.
B'Elanna Torres gets inside a Cardassian missile intended to destroy Maquis targets and reprograms it. She nicknames it Dreadnought.
Chakotay saves B'Elanna Torres's life, and she joins the Maquis.
Borum gives Rionoj Li Nalas' earring to smuggle out of the Hutet labor camp. Rionoj travels to Deep Space 9 and passes the earring to Quark, who later gives it to Kira Nerys.
By supporting a Bajoran extremist group called The Circle, the Cardassian Union tries to regain its influence on Bajor.
Cadet Harry Kim receives the Cochrane Medal of Excellence.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard successfully passes a test of Humanity's worth issued by the Q Continuum and prevents the destruction of the Human race by a temporal anomaly.
Deanna Troi is promoted to full commander and becomes a bridge officer upon passing the Bridge Officer's test.
Dr. Beverly Crusher discovers Barclay's Protomorphosis Syndrome when she inadvertently causes the Enterprise-D to become infected.
Edgar Willoughby, Jr. is born.

episodes.title: Treachery, Faith and the Great River

events.summary: Elaysian Ensign Melora Pazlar is transferred to Deep Space 9. Julian Bashir falls in love with her and plans a risky procedure based on the research of Nathaniel Teros to help her walk in normal gravity.
Ensign Sito Jaxa is believed killed during a mission to return a Cardassian officer working as a Federation spy to the Cardassian Union.
Harry Kim graduates from Starfleet Academy. He is "commissioned to rank of ensign."
Harry Kim's application to Voyager is denied.
In order to keep Opaka's role in the Kendra Valley Massacre secret, Bareil Antos withdraws from the kai election. Winn Adami is elected unopposed.
Jadzia Dax is reunited with Kang, Kor, and Koloth and joins them on a mission to kill an enemy who has eluded them for almost a century – The Albino.
Kathryn Janeway is given command of the USS Voyager.
Kim applies for service aboard Voyager. His application is approved.
Kim requests transfer to Starfleet Engineering Corps.
Kira Nerys and Julian Bashir are transported to the mirror universe.
Lorit Akrem dies in a mine collapse on Dozaria.

Miles O'Brien is captured and put on trial by the Cardassians, who claim he is a member of the Maquis. In reality, the Cardassians hoped to discredit the Federation and allow the Central Command to send ships into the DMZ and wipe out the Maquis.
Nog steals a springball racket from Major Kira Nerys' quarters.
Quark is executed. The primary Doctor Bashir kills Odo.
Ro Laren completes the Advanced Tactical Training program and is promoted to lieutenant. She returns to the Enterprise-D, but later defects to the Maquis.
Sisko takes Jake, Nog, and Quark to the Gamma Quadrant so that the two students can perform a planetary survey. Sisko and Quark are taken prisoner by the Jem'Hadar.
Sneed is arrested in Federation territory. He is found guilty of smuggling and sentenced to four years imprisonment.
Standard warp drive is discovered to have detrimental effects on the fabric of subspace. A program is launched to develop a safer means of warp travel, which is first incorporated on the Intrepid.
The Brunali homeworld is attacked by the Borg Collective.
The El-Aurian Martus Mazur sets up Club Martus on Deep Space 9's Promenade.

The Enterprise-D receives a new warp core on Thanatos VII, manufactured with interphasic techniques. The core also brings along interphasic organisms which attach themselves to the crew, but a method is later devised to remove them.
The Skrreea arrive from the Gamma Quadrant looking for the mythical planet Kentanna.
The Terran Rebellion begins.
The USS Equinox, commanded by Captain Rudolph Ransom, is commissioned.

The USS Pegasus is found in the Devolin system. The phasing cloak is retrieved by Admiral Erik Pressman and installed aboard the Enterprise-D to allow it to escape from within an asteroid. Illegal Starfleet involvement in the development and testing of the device is revealed.

Verad boards Deep Space 9, while the station is being operated by a skeleton crew, to steal the Dax symbiont from Jadzia Dax. He is later stopped by Benjamin Sisko and the symbiont is returned to Jadzia.
Wesley Crusher leaves Starfleet Academy to join the Traveler in explorations of alternate planes of reality.

While searching for suitable planets to establish colonies near the wormhole, Benjamin Sisko and Miles O'Brien discover a lost Erewon-class colony ship, and the remains of the colonists who have been living under an artificially created EM field for ten years. The leaders of the colony, Alixus and her son Vinod, who set up the field, are taken back to stand trial for the deaths of the colonists that could have been saved over the years.
William T. Riker and Deanna Troi observe the final adventure of the original Enterprise.
Zar begins his association with Dala, in which he will never have known her to perform menial labor for six years.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-fourth year in a row.
Zim Brott begins to serve aboard Deep Space 9.
According to William Riker, the fusion reactor in the Uxbridge house would last until this year.
Benjamin Sisko is killed by the Klingon-Cardassian Alliance. Miles "Smiley" O'Brien recruits the prime universe Benjamin Sisko to take his counterparts place temporarily.
Benjamin Sisko is promoted by Starfleet Command from commander to captain.
Kirk leaves the Nexus and dies helping Picard stop Soran from destroying the Veridian system.
Deep Space 9 is spared destruction by mere seconds after a legacy counter-insurgency program installed by the station's original Cardassian commander is inadvertently initiated.
Tolian Soran collapses the Amargosa star with a trilithium solar probe.
Ensign Samantha Wildman discovers she is pregnant while stranded on Voyager in the Delta Quadrant.
It is discovered that the Founders of the Dominion have infiltrated the Alpha Quadrant.
Jennifer Sisko defects to the Terran Rebellion.
Kar, a young Kazon-Ogla, begins his combat training on Tarok.

Kes travels to this year from five years in the original version of the future. She attempts to give the crew of Voyager to the Vidiians in exchange for safe passage for her future and present self to Ocampa. Her plan fails and Janeway is forced to kill Kes. Kes then makes a holographic projection to prevent her future self from going back in time, thus changing history.
Kira Nerys speaks to Latha Mabrin for what will ultimately be the last time.
Lurak T'Luco is released from prison. He had served five years for hijacking, theft, and assault.
Marina Derex's Labyrinths of the Mind is published.
Martok is captured by the Dominion on Kang's Summit and sent to Internment Camp 371.
Neelix and Kes join the Voyager crew.

Originally, Voyager discovered the class M planet devastated by an explosion that happened the previous day due to the detonation of polaric ion energy. Captain Janeway and Tom Paris were thrown back in time before the explosion by a subspace fracture. The Voyager crew attempts to rescue them by opening a fracture with a polaric generator but cause the original explosion and destroy all life on the planet, including the captain. Janeway resets time by closing the fracture before it causes the explosion. This creates a new timeline in which Voyager never went to the planet and the civilization is spared.
Quark visits his family home for the first time in twenty years.
Reginald Barclay transfers to Jupiter Station and works at Dr. Lewis Zimmerman's holographic laboratory to test the Emergency Medical Hologram's interpersonal skills.
A fire at the family home in La Barre kills Robert Picard and his son René.
Seska's true roots as a Cardassian spy are revealed. She betrays the Voyager crew and joins the Kazon.
Several crewmembers on Voyager, including first officer Cavit, flight control officer Stadi, the chief medical officer, and the chief engineer, are killed during the transit to the Delta Quadrant.
Smiley steals the design schematics for the USS Defiant during his visit to Deep Space 9.
Sometime around this year, variable geometry pylons are introduced on Federation starships.
Starfleet adopts a new combadge, a gold rectangle around a cut-out oval.
Steve Levy is promoted to Lieutenant.
The Bajoran Shakaar Edon, a former cell leader in the Bajoran Resistance, becomes first minister of Bajor's provisional government.

The Cardassian Obsidian Order, together with the Romulan Tal Shiar, are lured into a trap when they attack the Founders' homeworld in the Gamma Quadrant. The following battle leads to the complete destruction of the combined Romulan-Cardassian fleet of twenty ships and severely cripples both organizations, which, in turn, reduces possible resistance against the Dominion down to the Federation and the Klingon Empire.
The Enterprise-D is lost over Veridian III, most of the crew surviving the saucer's crash landing.

The Federation, as well as the other Alpha and Beta Quadrant powers, becomes aware of the Dominion threat after the USS Defiant returns from its mission to the Founders' homeworld in the Gamma Quadrant, the home of the designated leaders of the Dominion.
The Maquis member Thomas Riker steals the USS Defiant and uses it to expose a Cardassian military buildup in the Orias system.
The Obsidian Order exposes Legate Tekeny Ghemor's ties to the Cardassian dissident movement.

The Romulan Star Empire sends Ruwon and Karina to Deep Space 9 to take possession of Starfleet's data on the Dominion. This was agreed upon earlier in the year, in exchange for allowing the USS Defiant to operate a cloaking device. The Romulans also send a Warbird, intending to destroy the station and the wormhole. However, their plan is thwarted by Miles O'Brien, who had foreseen the future and warned Benjamin Sisko.

The starship USS Equinox disappears, pulled into the Delta Quadrant by the Caretaker. The ship sets a return course to the Alpha Quadrant, but encounters the Krowtonan Guard, becoming severely damaged while losing half of its crew.

The starship USS Voyager departs Deep Space 9 on a mission to find the Maquis raider Val Jean. Both ships are thrown seventy-five thousand light years into the Delta Quadrant by the Caretaker. The Caretaker's array, the only means home, is later destroyed by Captain Kathryn Janeway in order to protect the Ocampa and the two crews are forced to work together to find a route back to the Alpha Quadrant.
The USS Enterprise undergoes a major refit.

The USS Olympia crashes on a planet in Rutharian sector. Captain Lisa Cusak's distress call is sent three years into the future by the planet's energy barrier and is intercepted by the USS Defiant. Captain Cusak dies on the planet.
Tuvok joins Chakotay's Maquis cell as an undercover operative of Starfleet.

Voyager approaches an M-class planet. Kes enters the bridge, reporting a vision of a planet being destroyed. She is relieved to find out that the planet is teeming with life. It appeared to be a pre-warp civilization, so Captain Janeway makes a note in the log and orders Voyager to continue towards the Alpha Quadrant.

Voyager discovers a micro-wormhole. The crew sends in a microprobe, which reveals the wormhole leads to the Alpha Quadrant. Voyager, using the probe as a relay for a message, contacts a Romulan science vessel named Talvath, commanded by Telek R'Mor. However, it is later found out that the wormhole is a rift through time and that R'Mor is from the year 2351. After some consideration, R'Mor agrees to take the crew's personal messages with him and deliver them to Starfleet at the appropriate time. R'Mor is later discovered to have died in 2367 and the messages were apparently never delivered to Starfleet.
Voyager lands on a planet in the Delta Quadrant and discovers a colony of Humans, including Amelia Earhart, transported to the planet by the Briori centuries prior.
Voyager makes first contact with the Ocampa, Talaxians, and Kazon.
Voyager’s Tom Paris is convicted of murdering the Banean Tolen Ren. After much evidence gathering, Tuvok is able to prove Paris' innocence and reveals a Numiri plot to steal Ren's technology.
With help from Bareil Antos, Kai Winn and Legate Turrel sign the Bajoran-Cardassian Treaty. It sends political shock waves throughout the Alpha Quadrant. Bareil dies soon after.
Worf is promoted to lieutenant commander.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-fifth year in a row.
A Cardassian missile reprogrammed by B'Elanna Torres is found by Voyager in the Delta Quadrant, set for a course to destroy a planet. The missile is destroyed by Torres before it strikes the planet.

A member of the Q Continuum, Quinn, is released from his confinement in a comet by the crew of Voyager. After a brief trial, he relinquishes his powers so he can become Human and commit suicide. Q secures the poison used in the suicide. The event causes a civil war in the Continuum.
A transporter accident combines Tuvok and Neelix into a single being, Tuvix. He is later separated into his two halves against his will by Captain Janeway.

Admiral Leyton stages a military coup by taking advantage of the current Changeling scare. The attempt is thwarted by Benjamin Sisko and Odo. Leyton resigns his commission, and Sisko and Odo return to Deep Space 9.
After being convicted of espionage, Miles O'Brien is given the memories of a twenty-year prison sentence on Argratha.
An Annari warship commanded by General Valen becomes trapped in The Void.

Captain Benjamin Sisko takes the USS Defiant to the Founders' homeworld, hoping that the Founders will be able to cure Odo. It is revealed that the Founders had Weyoun infect Odo with the disease, necessitating his return. As punishment for killing a Changeling one year earlier, he is turned into a solid.

Captain Janeway and Commander Chakotay contract a virus from an insect bite and are forced to be left behind on the planet upon which they were infected. Tuvok becomes Voyager’s new commanding officer for six weeks until a cure is obtained from the Vidiians.

Captain Kathryn Janeway attempts to form an alliance with various Kazon sects to bring a stop to their increasingly frequent attacks on Voyager. Unfortunately, the negotiations are put to a stop after the Trabe attempt to wipe out the Kazon delegates.

Commander Chakotay discovers the Sky Spirits living on a planet in the Delta Quadrant, who visited Earth some forty-five thousand years before and reveals himself to be a descendant of the Inheritors.

Commander Chakotay is taken prisoner by the Kazon-Ogla after drifting into their space in a shuttlecraft while performing the pakra ceremony. He and a young Kazon boy named Kar escape a Kazon carrier vessel to the moon Tarok. Afterward, Kar earns his name after killing Jal Razik and Chakotay completes the pakra ceremony on the USS Voyager.

Convinced by a Changeling posing as General Martok that the Cardassian government had been infiltrated by the Founders, the Klingon Empire invades the Cardassian Union. However, the United Federation of Planets objects to this action, causing Chancellor Gowron to withdraw from the Khitomer Accords, ending their alliance. At the end of this year, the Changeling posing as General Martok convinces Gowron to take further aggressive action, causing the Klingon Empire to annex the Archanis sector from the Federation, thereby starting an open war between the two powers.

Doctor Julian Bashir is able to prevent unborn children of the people living on Boranis III from contracting a plague sent by the Dominion as punishment for the rejection of their offer of membership.
Greta Vanderweg is transferred to Deep Space 9.
Hector Ilario graduates from Starfleet Academy.
Jennifer Sisko is killed.
Kira Nerys begins a relationship with Shakaar Edon.
Kurn is given a new identity after the dishonor of the House of Mogh.

Michael Jonas begins secretly feeding the Kazon-Nistrim information about Voyager. Lieutenant Tuvok discovers the transmissions, but is unable to determine who sent them. To flush out the spy, Captain Janeway and Tuvok create a plan to have Tom Paris begin behaving like a malcontent to have a plausible reason for him to leave Voyager, be captured by Kazon forces, and extract information exposing the traitor.
Michael Jonas is revealed to be the traitor on Voyager supplying information to the Kazon. He is killed in a fight with Neelix.
Miles O'Brien and Julian Bashir are captured by a group of Jem'Hadar. Goran'Agar, their leader, orders Bashir to work on a cure for his men's addiction to ketracel-white.
Regent Worf attempts to retake Terok Nor from the rebels, but is defeated in battle by the newly-completed Defiant, commanded by Captain Sisko of the prime reality.
Unusual activity is picked up along the Romulan Neutral Zone.
Starfleet introduces styles of phasers and tricorders in this year.

The Antwerp Conference is bombed. An investigation reveals that the bomb was placed by a Changeling. Benjamin Sisko is temporarily reassigned to Earth to serve as Chief of Starfleet Security, in charge of preparing the Federation for a possible Dominion invasion.
The Bajoran wormhole undergoes an inversion that occurs once every fifty years.
The Doctor begins a brief relationship with the Vidiian Danara Pel.

The Kazon-Nistrim briefly steal transporter technology from Voyager. Chakotay is able to eliminate the Kazon's newfound technology and return to Voyager, but not before Seska informs him that she stole a sample of his DNA and impregnated herself with it.
The Klingon Empire attacks three Romulan outposts along the border.
The Korma Conference was scheduled to take place this year, however, the outpost hosting the conference was destroyed by a Klingon Bird-of-Prey that was later commandeered by Gul Dukat.
The Nanut was expected to end its two year mission to the Gamma Quadrant in this year.
The Terran Rebellion captures Terok Nor and begins constructing the ISS Defiant with the plans stolen from Deep Space 9 the previous year.
The USS Enterprise-E is launched from the San Francisco Fleet Yards.
The war between the Druoda and an unnamed species in the Delta Quadrant ends.

Violating the rules of Ferengi society, Quark's employees, led by Rom, form a labor union and go on strike. Quark secretly agrees to the union's demands to protect his brother, and the union is dissolved. Rom quits working for Quark and takes a position under Chief Miles O'Brien on Deep Space 9's engineering crew.

Voyager discovers a new type of dilithium which allows a shuttlecraft, piloted by Tom Paris, to achieve warp 10 flight. However, the effects of this type of transwarp are proved detrimental to humanoid DNA, and it is abandoned.

Voyager encounters a plasma drift which somehow duplicates every single molecule on board the ship. Severe damage is inflicted to one Voyager, and Harry Kim dies after being blown out through a hull breach. Naomi Wildman is born to Samantha Wildman during the incident. The other Voyager is later attacked by Vidiians, but is able to send Kim along with the Wildman child, who survived on this vessel, to the damaged Voyager. The other Voyager then self-destructs.
Voyager encounters the Caretaker's mate, Suspiria, and discovers that she is hostile.
Voyager is taken by the Kazon-Nistrim, assisted by Seska, and the crew is marooned on Hanon IV.

While escorting a medical convoy to Pentath III, Lieutenant Commander Worf accidentally destroys a Klingon civilian convoy. The Klingon Empire tries to have Worf extradited and charged for the deaths of the 441 civilians on board. Fortunately, it is revealed that the transport was empty and giving false readings to justify an armed conflict, and the casualty list was in fact a list of the victims of an accidental transport crash that occurred three months before this incident.
Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-sixth year in a row.
A Deep Space 9 salvage team led by Miles O'Brien visits Empok Nor.
A group of Talaxians lead by Oxilon establish an asteroid colony.
A Starfleet team lead by Benjamin Sisko are successful in capturing a Jem'Hadar attack ship.
After the real identity of the Klingon General Martok as a Changeling is revealed, the war between the Federation and the Klingon Empire is ended with a temporary cease fire.
B'Elanna Torres begins to have dreams that turn out to be memories implanted by an elderly Enaran woman.
General Martok is given command of the IKS Rotarran and is successful in rescuing survivors of the IKS B'Moth.
In the Delta Quadrant, the crew of Voyager discover a race called the Voth, a species that evolved on Earth and migrated to the Delta Quadrant several million years ago.
Jake Sisko is successful in obtaining a Willie Mays baseball card for his father Benjamin.
Julian Bashir is replaced by a Changeling.
Keiko O'Brien is inhabited by a Pah-wraith who tries to kill the Prophets, but is stopped by Miles O'Brien. The entity is expelled from Keiko's body.
Kirayoshi O'Brien, the second child of Miles and Keiko O'Brien, is born.
Klingon Chancellor Gowron reinstates the Khitomer Accords with the Federation.

Lieutenant Tuvok began suffering from a memory virus accidentally transferred to his mind from Dimitri Valtane eighty years previously, in 2293. With Kathryn Janeway acting as his pyllora, the two try to discover the cause of the virus and inadvertently experience memories of Tuvok's first Starfleet posting – the USS Excelsior under the command of Captain Hikaru Sulu.
Quark and Odo get stranded on a remote class L planet and must work together to survive and get back to Deep Space 9.
Silaran Prin begins his revenge on the Shakaar resistance cell. He kills the majority of its former members but is stopped by Kira Nerys.
Starfleet declares Voyager lost with all hands; Janeway begins writing letters home.
Starfleet rolls out a new uniform, with quilted grey shoulders and division colour on the undershirt and cuffs.
Tekeny Ghemor visits Deep Space 9 and undergoes the Shri-tal ritual. He dies shortly after.
The Bashir Changeling is exposed and killed. The real Bashir resumes his post on Deep Space 9 after a five-week confinement in Internment Camp 371.
The Borg and Species 8472 go to war after the Borg attempt to invade Species 8472's native fluidic space.
The Cardassian Union officially joins the Dominion.
The Doctor uncovers that Commander Chakotay did not father Seska's child, as originally believed. It is revealed that Maje Culluh is the child's true father.
The Dominion invades the Alpha Quadrant.
The Dominion signs non-aggression treaties with several powers, including the Romulans, Tholians, Miradorn, and Bajorans.
The Dominion War begins.
The Klingons invade Ajilon Prime.

episodes.title: Nor the Battle to the Strong

events.summary: The macrovirus infects the crew aboard Voyager and Kathryn Janeway must find a way to restore her crew.

The planet Gaia is discovered. Several weeks later the quantum energy barrier that encases the planet becomes impossible to get through, making the planet inaccessible. In an alternate timeline, the Defiant is sent two hundred years backward through time by the barrier, causing the crew of the ship and their descendants to colonize the planet.
The Q Civil War ends in this year with the birth of Q.

The Second Battle of Deep Space 9 takes place, during which the Dominion takes control of the Bajor sector, including the Federation space station Deep Space 9 at its crucial position next to the wormhole to the Gamma Quadrant.
The Borg's second incursion is broken at the Battle of Sector 001, and the Enterprise-E follows a sphere back to 2063.

The USS Defiant and its crew are transported back in time to the 23rd century, where they encounter the crew of the USS Enterprise and are successful in stopping Arne Darvin from killing James T. Kirk.
The USS Farragut is destroyed by the Klingons while protecting the colonists of Ajilon Prime.

episodes.title: Nor the Battle to the Strong

events.summary: The USS Voyager is retaken by Tom Paris and a group of Talaxians, led by Commander Paxim. Seska is killed during the operation. Voyager’s crew is recovered from Hanon IV. It moves beyond the Kazon Collective space once it goes to maximum warp in a nearby nebula.
Vorik begins undergoing pon farr and initiates a mating bond with an unwilling B'Elanna Torres.
Voyager enters the Nekrit Expanse.

Voyager is thrown into the year 1996 after encountering the timeship Aeon. The ship is later returned to this year but brings along a mobile emitter which allows The Doctor to operate in areas without a holographic generator.
With the Dominion as their new ally, the Cardassian Union successfully puts an end to the Maquis rebellion in the Demilitarized Zone.
Worf, Jadzia Dax, Quark, Julian Bashir, and Leeta visit Risa.

episodes.title: Let He Who Is Without Sin...

events.summary: Zek wins the Global Tongo Championship for the twenty-seventh year in a row, securing a comeback victory with the aid of Ishka's advice.
Alexander Rozhenko transfers to the IKS Rotarran, where he and his father Worf reconcile.

An alien named Arturis attempts to trap the crew of Voyager on a phony Starfleet vessel called the USS Dauntless. His deception is revealed, but the Voyager crew learns the use of quantum slipstream drive technology, which allows them to travel several hundred light years closer to home.
B'Elanna Torres undergoes the Klingon Day of Honor ritual.
Bareil Antos and Intendant Kira cross over from the mirror universe to steal an Orb.

Benjamin Sisko leads a team to destroy a ketracel-white facility aboard the captured Jem'Hadar attack ship they salvaged a year previously. They crash on a rogue planet in a dark matter nebula, where they encounter several Jem'Hadar and their Vorta. Keevan betrays the Jem'Hadar and Sisko and his team are able to escape.
Dukat escapes Federation custody just before his war crime trial is due to begin. He vows to destroy Bajor.
Gabriel Hwang is born on Hellas Planitia to Jae Hwang and Raffaela Musiker.
Grand Nagus Zek is briefly deposed, but is soon reinstated thanks to Quark's female alter ego Lumba.

In an alternate timeline, Voyager is attacked by Krenim forces shortly after the activation of the astrometrics lab. For nearly a year, the ship is under constant attack and becomes barely functional, forcing most of its crew to abandon ship. The timeline was restored after Voyager rammed and destroyed Annorax's weapon ship.
In First Battle of Chin'toka, a combined Federation, Klingon, and Romulan fleet successfully invade the Chin'toka system.
Investigating a subspace compression, the Defiant is taken over by the Jem'Hadar. Fortunately, the miniaturized USS Rubicon is able to stop them before they reach a Dominion base.
Kes' psionic abilities are hyperaccelerated by telepathic contact with Species 8472 and she leaves Voyager, pushing the ship clear of Borg space and 9,500 light years closer to Earth.
Miles O'Brien infiltrates the Orion Syndicate and befriends Liam Bilby. He discovers that the Syndicate is working with the Dominion.
Molly O'Brien falls through a time portal on Golana. She is brought back, but ages eight years. She is later returned to normal.
Morn fakes his own death to ensure his former partners in the Lissepian Mother's Day Heist are caught.
Neral becomes Praetor of the Romulan Star Empire.
Omega molecules are produced by a civilization in the Delta Quadrant. The molecules are retrieved by Voyager, and Seven of Nine is able to stabilize the molecules shortly before they are destroyed.

Operation Return, one of the greatest engagements of the Dominion War, takes place. The Bajor sector, including the Federation space station Deep Space 9, is recaptured by combined Federation and Klingon forces.
Quark's mother Ishka is taken prisoner by the Dominion on a trip to Vulcan. Quark assembles a team comprising of himself, Rom, Nog, Brunt, Leck, and Gaila and successfully rescue her on Empok Nor.
Reginald Barclay leaves the USS Enterprise and moves to San Francisco. He later begins work on the Pathfinder Project.
Section 31 agent Luther Sloan attempts to recruit Julian Bashir into the organization.
Sneed is released from prison. He had served four years for smuggling.
The Annari Empire starts a blockade of the Kraylor homeworld.
Voyager helps the Borg drive Species 8472 back into fluidic space, gaining Seven of Nine in the aftermath.
The Doctor and B'Elanna Torres find themselves at the mercy of an insane hologram.

The Dominion introduces a new class of Jem'Hadar battleship. The USS Valiant attempts to exploit a design flaw in one of the battleships, but the attempt fails and the Valiant is destroyed with relative ease.
The Dominion invades and occupies Betazed.
The Evora reach warp capability.
At the Nel Bato Conference, Picard meets Hars Adislo and his paper on thermionic transconductance.

The Reckoning begins with the discovery of a Kosst Amojan tablet on Bajor. The havoc is eventually halted by Kai Winn Adami's intervention, but not until disasters strike across Bajor, including a tornado at Tamulna.

The Romulan Star Empire joins the Federation and the Klingon Empire in their war against the Dominion after the Romulans discover questionable but sufficient evidence of a planned Dominion sneak attack on the Star Empire obtained by Senator Vreenak. In reality, Benjamin Sisko, Elim Garak, and Grathon Tolar were involved in creating the evidence.
The Seventh Fleet fails to stop the Dominion in the Tyra system. Only fourteen ships from a fleet of one hundred twelve make it back to Federation lines.

The USS Defiant responds to a distress call from Captain Lisa Cusak of the USS Olympia. Although it was discovered that she had been dead for years, she had a great impact on Deep Space 9's senior staff.
The USS Defiant successfully destroys a Dominion sensor array in the Argolis Cluster.

The USS Protostar commanded by Captain Chakotay and Commander Adreek-Hu arrives back in time from 2436 through the Solum wormhole and crash lands on a desolate planet where it spends the next ten years marooned.

episodes.title: Last Flight of the Protostar, Part I

events.summary: Through the actions of Dukat, a Pah-wraith is released and enters the Bajoran wormhole, sealing it, and cutting the Bajoran people off from the Prophets. Dukat kills Jadzia Dax in the process.
Tuvok is promoted to lieutenant commander.
Vic Fontaine is activated for the first time on Deep Space 9. With his help, Odo and Kira Nerys begin dating.
Vice Admiral Fujisaki dies of food poisoning, although Section 31 suspects foul play.
Voyager briefly reaches the Alpha Quadrant over a Hirogen network, and some of the crew receive letters from home.
Voyager crewmembers Seven of Nine and Harry Kim build an astrometrics laboratory with Borg-inspired technology, which allows the ship to chart a more efficient course to the Alpha Quadrant.

Voyager is attacked and boarded by the Hirogen, who wish to use the ship's holodecks for holographic battles. They use the crew as participants in the simulations and reassign their identities to fit the scenario. The ship is later returned to control of the Starfleet crew in exchange for a holographic generator.

Voyager passes through a Mutara class nebula, a transit which takes approximately one month. All crew except for Seven of Nine and The Doctor are placed in stasis to counteract the detrimental effects of the nebula on organic tissue.

Voyager’s crew is duplicated by the "Silver Blood" on a class Y planet. Shortly after their departure, the duplicate crew leaves the planet and eventually loses memory of their origins, setting a course for the Alpha Quadrant.
Worf and Jadzia Dax are married in a traditional Klingon wedding ceremony on Deep Space 9.

A defective clone of the Vorta Weyoun attempts to defect to the Federation, but ultimately sacrifices himself to save Odo's life. Before he dies, he tells Odo that a sickness (later identified as the morphogenic virus) is spreading through the Great Link and is killing the Founders.

episodes.title: Treachery, Faith and the Great River

events.summary: A jack-in-the-box in the Bashir 62 holoprogram causes Vic Fontaine to lose control of his casino to Frank Chalmers. The Deep Space 9 crew stage a daring heist to steal it back from the mob. The plan is successful and Vic's Lounge returns to normal.

A joint Federation/Romulan Conference is held on Romulus. With the co-operation of Admiral William Ross, and the reluctant assistance of Julian Bashir, Section 31 uses the conference to meddle in Romulan politics and ensure that Kimara Cretak does not get a seat on the Continuing Committee.
According to Quark, by this year Broik would have paid off a six year debt after buying useless Yamok sauce in 2369.
Admiral Dougherty and the Son'a try to remove the Ba'ku to harvest their rings' metaphasic radiation, and Picard intervenes.

Aging Klingon legend Kor seeks Worf's help to obtain a command so that he can feel useful in the Dominion War. He later stops Worf from commanding a suicide mission against ten Jem'Hadar ships, instead leading the mission himself, thus stepping out of life and into history in a blaze of glory.
At the request of Chancellor Martok, Worf becomes the Federation ambassador to Qo'noS.
B'Elanna Torres refuses to take medical treatment that will save her life from a holographic representation of infamous Cardassian Doctor Crell Moset.
B'Elanna Torres suffers severe internal injuries from running high risk holoprograms with the safety protocols off.
Benjamin Sisko (accompanied by his son, father, and Ezri Dax) finds the Orb of the Emissary. The Bajoran wormhole is reopened and Sisko returns to Deep Space 9.
Benjamin Sisko and Kasidy Yates are married aboard Deep Space 9.

Benjamin Sisko buys twelve hecapates of land on Bajor, and makes plans to build a house there for his retirement. He asks Kasidy Yates to marry him and, after she accepts, asks his son, Jake, to be best man. Sisko is later warned by the Prophets not to go ahead with the marriage.
By this year, piracy becomes far less common among the Orion people.

Captain Benjamin Sisko is lost, presumed dead, in the Fire Caves on Bajor, together with the former Cardassian leader, Dukat, and the Bajoran spiritual leader, Kai Winn Adami. The Bajorans begin the process of electing a new kai. Benjamin Sisko later appears to his wife, Kasidy Yates-Sisko, and tells her that he is with the Prophets in the Celestial Temple.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard is awarded The Circle of Galaxies.
Captain Kathryn Janeway learns that a family tale about one of her ancestors, Shannon O'Donnel, has been exaggerated.
William Riker and Deanna Troi resume their relationship.

Damar's Cardassian Rebellion is crushed, but Cardassian citizens rise up against the Dominion. In response, the Jem'Hadar begin to destroy Cardassian cities, killing millions of people. Damar is killed in an attempt to storm Dominion Headquarters.
Determined to free his world from Dominion occupation, Damar forms and leads the Cardassian Rebellion.
Doctor Julian Bashir performs an operation on Sarina Douglas that frees her from her catatonic state.
Dukat is surgically altered to resemble a Bajoran.

Elim Garak suffers an emotional breakdown under the pressure of helping Starfleet to wage war against his Dominion-occupied homeworld. Ezri Dax helps him to recover, and subsequently decides to remain on Deep Space 9 to become the station's counselor.
Ezri Tigan is joined to the Dax symbiont.
Following the end of the Dominion War and the liberation of Cardassia, Elim Garak remains on Cardassia, his exile from his homeworld now officially over.
Gabriel Hwang is vaccinated for the Andorian flu, Tetanus, and Typhoid diseases.
General Martok assumes the leadership of the Klingon Empire as Chancellor of the Klingon High Council after Lieutenant Commander Worf kills former Chancellor Gowron in a duel.
entities: Chancellor of the Klingon High Council

events.summary: Grand Nagus Zek crosses over to the mirror universe and is captured by the Klingon-Cardassian Alliance. Quark and Rom are able to free him by giving Regent Worf a cloaking device from the IKS Rotarran. Worf's plan to attack Terok Nor fails and he is captured by the Terran Rebellion.

In an alternate timeline, Voyager is destroyed as a result of problems with the slipstream. The Delta Flyer, which had attempted to map the slipstream before Voyager, returns to Earth, with Harry Kim and Chakotay.

In an alternate timeline, Voyager is destroyed by a temporal disruptor planted by Captain Braxton from the 29th century. Fortunately, the crew of the USS Relativity, aided by Seven of Nine from this year, prevent the disaster.

In league with the surgically altered Dukat, and under the influence of the Pah-wraiths, Kai Winn Adami consults the ancient and forbidden Book of the Kosst Amojan in order to learn how to release the Pah-wraiths from the Bajoran Fire Caves.
Jadzia Dax, Vic Fontaine, Brunt, and Elim Garak are killed.

Julian Bashir uses illegal Romulan mind probes to extract information about the morphogenic virus from the mind of Section 31 operative Luther Sloan; Sloan commits suicide during the procedure. Bashir subsequently produces a cure, and successfully administers it to Odo.
Kasidy Yates-Sisko announces that she is pregnant with Benjamin Sisko's child.
Kira Nerys is given a temporary commission of Starfleet commander and, together with Odo and Elim Garak, travels to Cardassia to assist Damar with the Cardassian Rebellion against the Dominion.
Kira Nerys is transported to Empok Nor, where she discovers that Dukat is now the leader of the Cult of the Pah-wraiths.
Lieutenant Chu'lak carries out a spree of murders aboard Deep Space 9 before he is stopped by Lieutenant Ezri Dax, who has psychological help from murderous former host Joran Dax.
Lurak T'Luco is arrested on Remus. He is charged with possession of stolen firearms and dangerous materials and devices. The charges are dismissed due to insufficient evidence.

Miles O'Brien disappears on New Sydney while trying to find Morica Bilby. Ezri Dax returns home to Sappora VII to use her mother Yanas' influence to help find him. She discovers that her brothers are involved with the Orion Syndicate, and that her younger brother Norvo murdered Bilby's wife.

Nog returns to Deep Space 9 from Starbase 235, where he has received counseling and a replacement leg. Utterly unable to cope with life after AR-558, he retreats into the holographic refuge of Vic Fontaine's lounge.
Odo discovers another of the Hundred, Laas. The Changeling lives on Deep Space 9 for a time, before killing a Klingon. Kira Nerys helps him escape.

Odo learns that he is infected with the same morphogenic virus that is killing the Founders. Doctor Julian Bashir discovers that the virus was created by Section 31, and that they used Odo to transmit it to the Great Link.
Odo leaves the Alpha Quadrant to rejoin the Founders in the Great Link, curing them of the morphogenic virus.
Richard Bashir is released from prison this year.
Rom succeeds Zek as Ferengi Grand Nagus.

Species 8472 creates a simulation of Earth on a space station in the Delta Quadrant to train individuals of their species to infiltrate Starfleet Command. The USS Voyager later convinces them that the Federation is not a threat.

The alternate Voyager created by the "Silver Blood" of the class Y planet begins to lose molecular cohesion as a result of exposure to a new enhanced warp drive. The crew is able to realize their predicament, but Captain Kathryn Janeway refuses to seek shelter at a Y-class world until it is too late. The crew attempts to launch a message drone with information about their history, but the drone is destroyed and the ship disintegrates before it can contact the real Voyager.
The Breen attack on Earth takes place, in which Earth, specifically San Francisco, is attacked and severely damaged by Breen forces.
The Breen Confederacy becomes formally allied with the Dominion.
The Brunali homeworld is attacked by the Borg Collective.
The chief of operations of Deep Space 9, Miles O'Brien, and his family leave the station to return to Earth; O'Brien takes up a position at Starfleet Academy as a professor of engineering.

The Dominion War comes to an end after the alliance of the Federation, the Klingon Empire, and the Romulan Star Empire, together with the turncoat Cardassian fleet, takes control of the Cardassian system at the Battle of Cardassia. In the captured Dominion Headquarters, Odo cures the Female Changeling of the morphogenic virus and she subsequently orders Dominion forces to surrender.
The Evora become a Federation protectorate.

The IKS Rotarran destroys the Monac shipyard by triggering a solar plasma ejection after Worf, Martok, Miles O'Brien, Julian Bashir, and Quark undertake the mission in the late Jadzia Dax's name in order to enable her spirit to enter Sto-vo-kor.
The Phage has supposedly been cured during this year by the Think Tank.

The Romulan Star Empire constructs a hospital on one of Bajor's moons, Derna, but also equips it with several thousand plasma torpedoes. Colonel Kira Nerys leads a blockade of the moon in protest, and the situation is eventually resolved when Starfleet Admiral William Ross threatens the Romulans with Federation intervention.
The USS Defiant is destroyed by a Breen energy-dampening weapon in the Second Battle of Chin'toka.

The USS Defiant visits AR-558 on the front line of the battle with the Dominion. Upon seeing the losses the Starfleet unit stationed there has taken, Benjamin Sisko decides to stay until further reinforcements arrive. They are successful in holding a captured Dominion communications array at AR-558, but take heavy losses. Nog loses a leg after being shot by a Jem'Hadar soldier.
The USS Sao Paulo is renamed Defiant under Benjamin Sisko.
The Voyager crew builds the Delta Flyer shuttlecraft.

Tom Paris is demoted to the rank of ensign for insubordination and serves thirty days in the brig in connection with an unauthorized circumvention of the Prime Directive at a planetoid occupied by the Moneans and the resultant damage to the Delta Flyer.

USS Voyager encounters the USS Equinox, another Starfleet vessel stranded in the Delta Quadrant. The Equinox crew had previously broken the Prime Directive and killed several nucleogenic lifeforms for use as a fuel source to power an enhanced warp drive. The Equinox eventually deserts Voyager after stealing a new shield emitter designed to prevent the lifeforms from attacking. Voyager is attacked by the aliens.
Voyager attempts to use quantum slipstream drive once again. The ship travels ten thousand light years before the slipstream collapses. The technology is deemed too dangerous for repeated use.
Voyager enters a sparsely-populated region of space called the Void. While there, they discover a subspace vortex which brings them two thousand light years closer to home.

Voyager raids a Borg sphere and steals a transwarp coil, which allows it to travel twenty thousand light years. Seven of Nine is lured back to the Borg Collective by the Borg Queen, but she is later rescued by the Voyager crew.
Vulcan Captain Solok challenges old rival Benjamin Sisko to a game of baseball. Sisko's "Niners" team is defeated by Solok's "Logicians" in a match in one of Quark's holosuites.

episodes.title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite

events.summary: Worf takes command of the IKS Koraga, which is later destroyed. He manages to leave the ship in an escape pod, and is rescued by Ezri Dax. However, they are subsequently captured by the Breen.
After a near death experience, B'Elanna Torres travels to Gre'thor to rescue her mother Miral from the Barge of the Dead.

After five years, the Pathfinder Project of Starfleet's Communications Research Center manages to establish contact with Voyager, thanks to the efforts of Reginald Barclay. Eventually, Voyager can transmit and receive monthly data streams from the Alpha Quadrant.
Benjamin Sisko's child with Kasidy Yates was to have been born in this year.
Captain Kathryn Janeway is abducted by the Kellidians, but is rescued by Lieutenant Commander Tuvok.
During this year, Kes returns to Voyager. She attempts to travel five years back in time, but stops and decides to return to Ocampa.
Had the Federation won the trade agreement with the Barzan, the agreement would have ended this year.

In an alternate timeline, Kes returns to Voyager and destroys her ship in a collision with Voyager. She then damages Voyager, kills B'Elanna Torres, and uses Voyager’s warp core to travel back to 2371.
Research begins on expanding The Doctor's program into an Emergency Command Hologram, which will allow him to function as acting captain of Voyager in an emergency.
The Ocampan city is presumed to have run out of the surplus of power that the Caretaker sent to them before he died.

The USS Voyager pursues the USS Equinox and negotiates a cease fire with the nucleogenic lifeforms. The Equinox is later destroyed with only five crewmembers surviving; they are stripped of rank and ordered to serve as crewmen on Voyager, with extensive supervision and limited privileges.
The Voyager crew rescues four adolescent Borg drones from a derelict cube: Icheb, Mezoti, Azan, and Rebi.
Voyager encounters a graviton ellipse and retrieves data about the phenomenon from the command module of the Ares IV spacecraft, which had been trapped in the ellipse since 2032.
Voyager helps revive the Vaadwaur species. They later turn out to be hostile but their subspace corridors propel Voyager two hundred light years closer to the Alpha Quadrant.
Voyager uses a catapult designed by an alien called Tash to travel thirty sectors closer to Earth.

A group of Klingons who left Qo'noS in the 23rd century believe B'Elanna Torres' unborn child to be the Kuvah'magh, the prophetical "savior of their people." The child's DNA is used to cure a virus plaguing the Klingons, and they settle on a planet similar to Qo'nos.
Azan and Rebi are reunited with their people, who also agree to give Mezoti a home.
B'Elanna Torres discovers she is pregnant.

Ensign Harry Kim briefly commands a Kraylor vessel, with the understanding that it is a medical courier. It is revealed that the vessel is carrying a prototype cloaking device midway through the journey to the Kraylor homeworld, and although hesitant, Kim successfully commands the vessel to its destination.
Icheb applies to Starfleet Academy and begins taking the necessary courses on board Voyager. Captain Janeway sponsors his application.
Janeway and Chakotay die in an alternative reality.

Teero Anaydis, a Bajoran Maquis fanatic, uses a mind control program to attempt to revive the Maquis rebellion on the USS Voyager, causing the vessel's former Maquis crew members to seize control of the ship. Tuvok is able to reverse the effects via mind melds.
The crew of Voyager is abducted by a secret criminal faction of Quarren. Their identities are reassigned to that of laborers on the planet, but they are eventually rescued.

The Delta Flyer is destroyed by the Borg Tactical Cube 138. Captain Kathryn Janeway, Lieutenant Commander Tuvok, and Lieutenant B'Elanna Torres are assimilated by the Borg to assist the drones of Unimatrix Zero in their rebellion. The Borg drones capable of entering Unimatrix Zero begin a rebellion against the Collective, aided by the USS Voyager.
The Doctor is transmitted to the Alpha Quadrant in order to treat the terminally ill Doctor Lewis Zimmerman.

The original Delta Flyer is rebuilt as a new Delta Flyer. Janeway consents to permit Tom Paris to use the Delta Flyer in the Antarian Trans-stellar Rally. Paris loses the race after a plot is discovered and thwarted to sabotage the Delta Flyer to commit an assassination.
Tom Paris and B'Elanna Torres are married, and take their honeymoon on the Delta Flyer.
Tom Paris is returned to the rank of lieutenant junior grade.
Voyager encounters a temporal distortion which briefly splits the ship up into several different time periods.
Voyager is trapped in a region known as the Void, and forges alliances with several species in order to escape.
When they entered the Void in 2375, Voyager expected to exit it by this year.
A future version of Kathryn Janeway arrives from the year 2404 to aid Voyager in using the Borg transwarp network to return to the Alpha Quadrant.
A letter dictated by Tom Paris in 2375 is automatically transmitted from his personal database to Admiral Owen Paris when Voyager arrives back in the Sol Sector.
Admiral Slattery became so intoxicated at the Command Conference after-party that he commandeered a light cruiser and made it "halfway to the Delta Quadrant" before he sobered up.
Captain Janeway is captured by former members of the Hierarchy; Voyager’s EMH masquerades as the captain in order to save her and protect the crew.
Communications between USS Voyager and the Alpha Quadrant improve when real-time communication is made possible.
In an alternate timeline, Andrew Kim is born to Harry Kim and Linnis Paris.
Lieutenant Ayala transfers from the operations division to the command division aboard Voyager.
Lieutenant Joe Carey is murdered while on an away mission to retrieve the Friendship 1 probe, which had contaminated an unidentified planet in the Delta Quadrant.
B'Elanna Torres and Tom Paris's daughter Miral is born as Voyager reaches home.
Neelix departs Voyager to live with a group of Talaxians living in an asteroid field; he becomes the permanent Federation ambassador in the Delta Quadrant.
O'Connor begins to work on a sand mandala as part of his efforts to ascend.

One of six Borg transwarp hubs is destroyed by the crew of Voyager during her successful return trip home. The future Admiral Janeway remains in the Delta Quadrant to introduce a neurolytic pathogen into the Collective. The assault cripples the Borg and makes Voyager’s escape and use of the transwarp hub possible.
Q leaves his son temporarily in the custody of Kathryn Janeway in the hope that her Starfleet ideals will correct his negative behavior.
Seven of Nine and Chakotay begin dating.
The USS Voyager is decommissioned.
Voyager detects high levels of neutrino emissions inside a nebula in the Delta Quadrant. A large Borg presence is found inside and the crew initially avoids it.
Shinzon's death clears the way for continued Federation–Romulan rapprochement.
Beckett Mariner is demoted to ensign and transferred from the USS Quito to the USS Cerritos.
Brad Boimler is commissioned and later assigned to the Cerritos.
Picard is sent to Romulus after a coup installs a new government offering peace.
Evidence suggests Wesley Crusher had returned to Starfleet as a lieutenant junior grade by this year.

In an alternate timeline aboard USS Voyager in the Delta Quadrant, Kes nearly dies at the age of 9, experiences a temporal flux, and begins to travel back in time to the year of 2373. At that point in time she is put in a bio-temporal chamber, reactivating dormant chronitons in her body. She experiences temporal reversion as she time travels.
Kathryn Janeway serves as a vice admiral at Starfleet Command.
Sam Rutherford is transferred from Douglas Station to the Cerritos on Stardate 56329.4.
The damaged Enterprise-E is towed home to Earth for repair.
The shuttlecraft Argo is first tested on Kolarus III, where Soong's prototype B-4 is found.
Seven starships form Battle Group Omega to reinforce the Enterprise.
The USS Quito conducts first contact with the Galardonians.
Riker is promoted to captain of the Titan, with Troi transferring as counsellor.
Riker and Troi marry on Earth; the Betazed ceremony is cut short by the summons to Romulus.
Yor travels from this year to the future of the prime universe.
Brad Boimler is promoted to Lieutenant junior grade and transferred to the Titan.

Captain Carol Freeman puts a new directive into effect on USS Cerritos that all crew must have their assignments completed within a strict amount of time, after learning that some of the crew are using "buffer time" to get away with not doing as much work as they should, and feeling that she was removed from the peace conference with Cardassians, making her think that Starfleet does not take her and her ship seriously. On the planet Gelrak V, Commander Jack Ransom and Ensign Mariner are imprisoned for giving the wrong idol to the natives. Ransom fights their champion and secures their freedom.
Captain Dayton is assigned to the Solvang following the destruction of the Rubidoux.

Captain Durango is at odds with Captain Freeman and orders his ship to take the lead in moving the generation ship, which causes to his ship to be destroyed, and the Cerritos to experience terraformation by the generation ship's molecular fluid. Captain Freeman and Lieutenant Mariner are able to stop the fluid from completely overtaking the Cerritos, and manage to beam the Merced crew to safety.
Captain Jean-Luc Picard receives the Dignified Person award from the government of Vulcan.
Ensign D'Vana Tendi begins serving on the USS Cerritos following her transfer from Outpost 79.

Ensign Mariner violates the Prime Directive to liberate a species of sentient lizard-like beings from enslavement under rat-like humanoids, and is later beamed back aboard the Cerritos and ordered to attend therapy from the ship's counselor, Doctor Migleemo.

Ensign Samanthan "Sam" Rutherford transfers to different departments on the Cerritos before realizing that Engineering is his true calling. He agrees to watch the Trivoli pulsar with Ensign Tendi on her PADD while he works.

Ensigns Bradward "Brad" Boimler and Beckett Mariner escort the Klingon General K'orin to Tulgana IV. Mariner and K'orin get drunk on the shuttle. The ensigns go in search of the General after he steals the shuttle. Eventually K'orin arrives at the embassy for the meeting.
If the Moneans did not stop extracting oxygen from the Waters, their planet would have lost cohesion by this year.
Lieutenant Mariner insults Admiral Vassery in front of Captain Freeman. She is removed from her post and demoted back to Ensign.

On K'Tuevon Prime, the crew of the Cerritos is seemingly put on trial for the supposed crimes of its senior officers. Ensigns Boimler, Mariner, Tendi, and Rutherford testify on behalf of the senior staff about prior events, up to and including a hostile encounter with the Clicket ship Tweerk, the theft of a Romulan Bird-of-Prey from a Vulcan museum, a mission to Romulus, and a surprise Gorn wedding. However, the "trial" turns out to be a party thrown by Imperium Magistrate Clar, in honor of the crew who had rescued him from the Romulans.
Sam Rutherford and D'Vana Tendi are seen attending a New Year's Eve party.

Some Starfleet vessels and facilities begin to use a new uniform design, with the department color moving back to prominence and indicated by the jacket and the trim on the boots. The jacket shoulder area returned to black, now trimmed in white.

The Cerritos assists the USS Merced with the relocation of an ancient generation ship. During the mission, Beckett Mariner disrespects Captain Carol Freeman, who as an act of revenge, temporarily promotes her to Lieutenant and assigns her to her senior staff as an operations officer.
The Cerritos assists the USS Vancouver in imploding the moon of Mixtus III before its decaying orbit can damage the planet.

The Cerritos checks in on the civilization on Beta III, and finds that Landru has regained control over the population after being left alone for over two hundred years. The crew of the Cerritos reaffirms the population's belief in the Federation and departs.
The Cerritos departs from Douglas Station to make second contact with the Galardonian High Council. Most of the crew is briefly overcome by a rage virus before Commander T'Ana, MD finds a cure.

The Cerritos is engaged by a Drookmani ship while attempting to prevent the Drookmani from salvaging Starfleet wreckage. The Cerritos is damaged in the encounter, but does not open fire on the Drookmani for risk of prompting a war. The Drookmani ship is eventually destroyed by a corrupted isolinear core.
The Cerritos undergoes a temporary change in command, and later rescues the crew of the USS Rubidoux from an unidentified spaceborne creature that had been growing within their ship's hull.
The Solvang encounters a mysterious ship in the Kalla system and is quickly destroyed, sending off a partial distress signal, which the Cerritos receives.

Upon arriving in the Kalla system, the Cerritos discovers the wreckage of the USS Solvang and is subsequently engaged by the mysterious ship as well, discovering it to be crewed by Pakleds. The Cerritos is boarded by a Pakled attack force and captain Carol Freeman is severely injured, giving command of the ship to ensign Mariner. Rutherford and Shaxs undergo a dangerous mission to destroy the Pakled ship, which is successful but costs Shaxs his life and Rutherford his memories, only for three more Pakled ships to appear and greatly damage the Cerritos. Acting captain Mariner was on the verge of ordering the crew to abandon ship when the USS Titan arrives, and greatly damages all three Pakled warships, forcing them to flee. The Cerritos undergoes major repairs at Starbase 77 before resuming service.

Aledo goes rogue due to "daddy issues" stemming from flawed artificial intelligence and attacks Douglas Station alongside its sister ships when Buenamigo attempts to rescind its autonomy. Buenamigo is killed in doing so, along with several other personnel and civilians, before the Cerritos lures the ships away using Ensign Rutherford as bait. Rutherford had initially programmed the AI and was the one to realize and alert Captain Freeman to the danger.
Beckett Mariner is wrongfully accused of trying to ruin the ship's reputation and transferred to Starbase 80 as punishment, leading to her resignation from Starfleet.
Beverly Crusher steps away from the Enterprise to bring up her son Jack.
Brad Boimler returns to the Cerritos as an ensign. However, the computer refuses to recognize him, leading to mishaps.

Captain Maier participates in mediating a conflict between a group of scientists and the Scrubble on Jengus IV while Ensigns Kearns, Cor'Dee and Young participate in dismantling of a scientific outpost.
Carol Freeman is on trial for the destruction of Pakled Planet. She is cleared of all charges when it stands revealed that the Pakleds destroyed their own world.
Chairman Siggi of the Collector's Guild requested Federation assistance to catalog and dispose of dangerous items in the late Kerner Hauze's collection. The Cerritos was assigned to the task.

Crew of the Cerritos are tasked with transporting a Doopler emissary to Starbase 25 for trade negotiations, they were briefly hampered when the emissary began to duplicate himself exponentially via embarrassment but Captain Carol Freeman discovered that, when she was no longer placating insecure Dooplers, that angering them is the key to them rejoining into a single being.
Due to a deal with her sister D'Erika, Lieutenant Tendi is forced to leave the Cerritos.

Due to the Pakleds continuing their unprovoked attacks on Federation trade routes, the Federation tries to attempt a cease-fire, with the Cerritos being sent on a diplomatic mission to the Pakled homeworld, which they call Pakled Planet. The Pakleds use this as a ruse to send their spy Rumdar to spy on the ship schematic by pretending to be a asylum seeker. This plan backfired with Captain Freeman ultimately learning that the Pakleds planned to smuggle a Varuvian bomb onto Earth.
During a first contact mission to Lapeeria, the Archimedes is struck by a wave of magnetized ionic plasma which disables the ship. The ship is later rescued by the USS Cerritos.
During a second contact mission on Apergos, Commander Jack Ransom is exposed to strange energies, and temporarily gains god-like powers.

Engages in a chase with the Cerritos which results in its sister ships being destroyed when the Cerritos ejects her warp core, followed by its own destruction when all other vessels of the California come to the aid of the Cerritos.

Engages in a second contact race with the USS Aledo to avoid decommissioning; the Aledo wins, but does so in a way that violates the Prime Directive, which Captain Carol Freeman uses in a call with Admiral Buenamigo to attempt to disqualify the Aledo.
Ferenginar, whose trade routes have been affected by the Nova One attacks, begins the process of joining the Federation under Captain Freeman's watch.
Geordi La Forge begins work restoring the USS Enterprise.
In an alternate timeline, Jake Sisko marries Korena.
In another alternate timeline, Seven of Nine dies aboard Voyager after being injured on an away mission.

In another alternate timeline, Starfleet Command has the deep space vessel assigned to search for the missing USS Voyager give up its search six years after it crash landed on an unidentified planet in the Takara sector.
entities: Unnamed Alpha and Beta Quadrant planets

events.summary: In later battled a Pakled Clumpship and forced the latter to retreat.
It continued to battle the Pakleds on the far side of the system from Starbase 25.
Jean-Luc Picard is promoted to Admiral and leaves the Enterprise.
Lieutenant junior grade Kayshon was assigned to the USS Cerritos as the ship's new head of security.
Lieutenant Shaxs returns from the dead due to reasons preferred to be kept secret. Ensign Sam Rutherford learns the secret and is traumatized.
Lieutenant Tom Paris visits the Cerritos. He accidentally gets into a fist fight with Boimler due to his accidental resemblance to a Kazon
Lures the USS Aledo, USS Dallas, and USS Corpus Christi away from Douglas Station after the Aledo murders Buenamigo and attacks the station, almost leading to her destruction.

Mariner rallies all other California vessels to protect the Cerritos from the Aledo, then returns to Starfleet and resumes her commission. T'Lyn, a Vulcan from the Sh'vhal, arrives on the Cerritos as an Ensign (provisional)
Rutherford is promoted to Lieutenant junior grade through past achievements.

episodes.title: I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee

events.summary: Several crewmembers watch Locarno's announcement of Nova Fleet, during which time Mariner denounces Locarno and flees with a Genesis Device. This prompts Captain Freeman to disobey orders not to rescue Mariner, which the entire crew backs up. In the process of obtaining a ship to breach the trynar shield, Freeman ends up opening diplomatic ties with the Orions. In addition, Mariner never fires on Nova Fleet, and the Cerritos’ arrival causes Nova Fleet to abandon Locarno for his erratic and controlling behavior, preventing the rescue from causing a diplomatic incident. These factors allow Freeman to escape disciplinary action after Mariner is rescued.
Shari yn Yem, Starfleet Drill instructor, conducts tests on the Cerritos, all of the tests set to fail in order to keep her job. Mariner and Freeman are able to expose this plot.
Starbase 25 hosts this year's Command Conference.

Starfleet assigns the Cerritos to take Nick Locarno into protective custody after learning that Nova One is targeting ex-Starfleet personnel. However, on New Axton, an away team led by Captain Freeman discover blueprints for Nova One, proving that Locarno is the mastermind behind the attacks. Simultaneously, Mariner, stranded on Sherbal V after an attempt to keep her out of danger goes awry due to Nova Fleet activity in the Sherbal system, is abducted by Locarno and brought to the Detrion system.

Starfleet's Shame airs and is viewed by the crew of the Cerritos, who realize too late that Mariner was not responsible for the negative impression. In response to this PR disaster, Starfleet decides to decommission the California.
Takes part in a second contact race with the Cerritos which it wins but violates the Prime Directive in the process, which Captain Freeman uses to call for its disqualification.
Thaddeus Troi-Riker is born.

The Cerritos accidentally discover an encounter between the Pakled Clumpship Pakled and the IKS Che'Ta' and are attacked. The Vulcan cruiser Sh'vhal arrives to aid them while Ma'ah slays his captain Dorg and takes over the Che'Ta, escaping.
The Cerritos becomes privy to intelligence regarding Nova One.

The Cerritos crew sets up Voyager for its final journey to Earth and the Fleet Museum when a Tak Takian macrovirus causes trouble and the ship is hijacked by various hologram programs. Rutherford stops the hijacking by infecting the bio-neural gel packs with Neelix's schplict-based brill cheese.
The Cerritos enters Bynar space to investigate the "destruction" of a Bynar ship, but later discover that Nova One is not destroying the ships it attacks but towing them away.

The Cerritos is tasked with ending a war on an unnamed planet caused by the supercomputer AGIMUS. Mariner and Boimler are tasked with sending it to the Daystrom Institute for storage. Lieutenant Commander Andy Billups finds himself in another plot to take the throne of his planet Hysperia by his mother, Queen Paolana.

The Cerritos is tasked with preparing USS Voyager for its final trip to Earth, then the Fleet Museum when a transporter accident fuses Lieutenant Commander Andy Billups and Doctor T'Ana into T'Illups. Fearing a reprise of Tuvix' fate, T'Illups attempts to make an army of hybrids, but is stopped. Boimler, Mariner and Tendi are promoted to Lieutenant junior grade while T'Lyn is made the same rank as a provisional for their actions in saving both ships.

The Cerritos rescues the USS Archimedes when a wave of magnetized ionic plasma disables the ship. Freeman is asked to perform first contact with the Lapeerians in Captain Sonya Gomez's stead. However, Freeman is subsequently arrested and charged with colluding with Klingon extremists to destroy Pakled Planet.

The Cerritos was sent to Frylon IV to investigate the reported sighting of a Mugato, a dangerous creature not indigenous to this planet. They discovered an illegal Ferengi poaching operation where they harvested the Mugato's horn. Both Boimler and Rutherford manage to reach a compromise with the Ferengi to change the poaching operation to a nature reserve by appealing to the Ferengi love of profit.

The ship is called in to help mediate a conflict between a group of scientists and the Scrubble on Jengus IV while Ensigns Beckett Mariner, Sam Rutherford and Brad Boimler remove psychic mines. This, along with Ensign D'Vana Tendi discovering odd readings on a rock, accidentally expose a plot between the two groups to obtain and sell Federation secrets on the black market

The ship's crew also investigated the Pakled takeover of a Varuvian mining colony on Karzill IV, but were unable to determine whether any other power was involved in the operation. During this mission, a transporter duplicate of Brad Boimler, William Boimler, was created. The duplicate remained on the Titan, while the original Boimler returned to the Cerritos.
