The station plays host to post-war trade negotiations between the Karemma and the Federation. The negotiations are nearly ruined when it is discovered Quark stole Karemma tech for a new type of replicator, but Captain Carol Freeman is able to rerail negotiations.
The Vau N'Akat known as The Vindicator arrives back in time from 2436 and begins infiltrating Starfleet, posing as Trill Ensign Asencia.
USS Aledo launched, defending the Cerritos from Breen. Vice Admiral Les Buenamigo introduces the Texas to Starfleet
Vice Admiral Les Buenamigo tricks the Cerritos into an encounter with the Breen in order to unveil the USS Aledo.
While engaging in combat with several Pakled battle harpies, the Titan enters an anomaly and undergoes gluonic disruption.
In an alternate reality where everything was purple, both the USS Enterprise and Lieutenant Commander Data were still in service, rather than both having been destroyed years earlier.
In an alternate timeline where Captain Benjamin Sisko was temporally displaced, Jake Sisko marries Korena.

Stardate 59393.7. The Cerritos is docked at the Cosmic Duchess, to both handle a swarm of seemingly rogue nanites (Which are in reality being controlled by an alternate universe microscopic version of the USS Endeavour) with a side mission of trying to find Admiral Milius who went AWOL because he resents being assigned to milking space whales of all kinds.
The Cerritos aids Targalus IX in dismantling their capitalist economic system following the planet's admittance into the Federation.
The Cerritos is briefly drawn through a quantum fissure into an parallel universe, where it encounters that universe's version of the Cerritos.
The Protostar launches from the San Francisco Fleet Yards under Chakotay, with Adreek-Hu as first officer.

episodes.title: Last Flight of the Protostar, Part I

events.summary: A temporal anomaly almost destroys the Protostar.
Dal and the escaped prisoners explore (and escape from) an unknown class M planet in the Hirogen star system.
Dal R'El, Rok-Tahk, Zero, Jankom Pog, and Murf discover the USS Protostar and escape from the Tars Lamora prison colony, with Gwyn as their captive.
The crew of the Protostar makes a rather disastrous first contact with the Cymari.
The Protostar crew enacts a daring plan to rescue the inhabitants of Tars Lamora.
With the aid of a hologram of Kathryn Janeway, Dal and the escaped prisoners aboard the Protostar escape the gravity well of a white dwarf in a binary system.
Asencia kills The Diviner.
Dal R'El and his crew begin their journey towards Federation space.
Dal R'el, Jankom Pog, Murf, Rok-Tahk and Zero are able to join Starfleet as Warrant officers.
Gwyndala returns to Solum.
Sneed is arrested on this space station. He is charged with arson and sentenced to three months imprisonment.
Sometime prior to this year, The Traveler's species left the known plane of existence.

episodes.title: The Devourer of All Things, Part II

events.summary: Starfleet learns that Captain Chakotay and his his crew are trapped 52 years into the future.
The USS Protostar is destroyed in an effort to destroy the Living construct.
The Voyager is launched on her maiden voyage under Vice admiral Kathryn Janeway.
Vice admiral Kathryn Janeway begins their search for Chakotay and the USS Protostar.
According to Section 31 estimates made in 2375, the Klingon Empire will have finally recovered from their losses incurred during the Dominion War.
Captain Chakotay is given command of the Voyager-A.
Synthetics compromised by the Zhat Vash set Mars ablaze and destroy the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards.
Eli Foster graduates from Starfleet Academy.

In the aftermath of this attack, Starfleet Command was asked to "scale back" operations, ceasing all exploratory missions and focusing on protecting Federation citizens. Admirals Janeway and Picard both opposed the move, but Fleet admiral Jellico stated in a meeting with several Starfleet Officers that, with the loss of the A500s, resource shortages, and a lack of engineers, that Starfleet's mission of "peace" would have to wait.

Starfleet abandons its plans to aid the Romulan evacuation, a decision leading Admiral Picard to resign his commission. The decision is informed by concerns that the Federation might implode over support for the Romulans, as even before the attack, fourteen species within the Federation have threatened to pull out over the issue. Also as a result of the attack, synthetic lifeforms are banned throughout the Federation and research is halted, which was the Zhat Vash’s original goal.
Starfleet is in the process of resettling Romulans threatened by an impending supernova. This includes the settlement of refugees on Vashti.
The crew stop Asencia's time-space weapons and return the Protostar to its place in the timeline.
The USS Prodigy is launched under the command of Acting captain Gwyndala and her first officer Dal R'El.
The Voyager makes official first contact with the Vau N'Akat.
Daughter is entered into storage in Picard's section of the quantum archive at the Starfleet Archive Museum. The painting is given the item number 227.67.
Icheb is stripped of his Borg components by Bjayzl on Vergessen, and Seven of Nine ends his suffering.
Jean-Luc Picard receives the Crystal Planet Award.
The vintage bottle of Chateau Picard wine which Jean-Luc Picard later brought to Raffi Musiker is manufactured.
Spock prepares a red-matter ship to collapse the Romulan supernova into an artificial black hole.
Spock's red matter neutralises the supernova, and the resulting black hole swallows his ship and Nero's.
The Romulan supernova destroys Romulus.
In an alternate timeline, the Collected Stories by Jake Sisko wins the Betar Prize.
Laris is forced to remind Jean-Luc Picard to wash his hands for the first of many times.
After being confronted by his first officer over his actions, Commander Cristóbal Rios, Vandermeer commits suicide.
Captain Alonzo Vandermeer of the USS Ibn Majid receives a black flag directive from Commodore Oh of Starfleet Security, ordering him to kill Beautiful Flower and Jana.
Jean-Luc Picard and Worf will last see one another for the next eleven years.
Rios follows through on the directive, purging the transporter log and dumping the bodies into space. Subsequently, he is given a medical discharge from Starfleet.
The last contact between the Federation and the Borg Collective "for over a decade" prior to 2401 takes place during or before this year.
The USS Ibn Majid encounters a ship in the Vayt sector. Aboard are two synths, Beautiful Flower and Jana.
By this year, Quark wouldn't have visited his family home for forty years, if he'd had his way.
Doctor Vellek secures his diary inside a tan zhekran.
In an alternate timeline, The Doctor invents the synaptic transceiver.
In an alternate timeline, USS Voyager returns to Earth after 23 years in the Delta Quadrant. Chakotay dies in the same year.
In another timeline, Naomi Wildman and Icheb assist Kathryn Janeway and Chakotay in preventing Voyager from going into temporal flux from a chronokinetic surge.
A music festival is held at Bewlay Island, Stardust City.
Picard gifted Commodore Geordi La Forge a bottle of Chateau Picard, the latter hating it and the former accusing the latter of having "pedestrian" tastes.
The events of the anti-time future are assumed, by Jean-Luc Picard, to have taken place in this year.
Doctor Agnes Jurati suspected that Dahj Asha's fictional identity was built around this year.
Jack Crusher attempts to make contact with Jean-Luc Picard at 10 Forward Avenue.
Liam Shaw assumes command of the Titan-A.
According to Narek, his brother dies unexpectedly.
A Romulan fleet, led by Oh, arrives at Coppelius. She is subsequently revealed to Starfleet to be a Tal Shiar/Zhat Vash double agent.
Admiral Jean-Luc Picard travels to Vashti on board the ship La Sirena to request assistance from the Qowat Milat.

After discovering that the Zhat Vash might be behind Dahj's assassination, Jean-Luc Picard requests to be reinstated into Starfleet to find Bruce Maddox. He is denied and goes off to recruit a crew of his own.
After retired Admiral Jean-Luc Picard gives his first interview, Dahj Asha seeks him out. She is subsequently killed by Romulan operatives.
After some delay, Doctor Naáshala Kunamadéstifee starts a residency at the Borg Artifact Research Institute by the Romulan Free State.
Bruce Maddox is killed by Agnes Jurati.
Criminal businesswoman Bjayzl is executed by Seven of Nine in revenge for Icheb's death thirteen years earlier.
Data's consciousness is terminated by Picard, twenty years after his death.
Fleeing the Artifact, Picard and Soji Asha take refuge with William Riker and Deanna Troi on Nepenthe. Hugh, director of the Borg Reclamation Project on the Artifact, is killed by Narissa.
Jean-Luc Picard dies during the Defense of Coppelius. His consciousness is subsequently transferred to a golem.
La Sirena, Narek's snakehead, and the Artifact are captured by Orchids and crash land on Coppelius.
The Federation's synth ban is lifted after fourteen years.
Deep Space 4 shuts down.
In an alternate timeline, Harry Kim is promoted to captain and given command of the USS Rhode Island. The Rhode Island departs on a four-year mission of exploration.
Sabrina is born to Naomi Wildman.
Zhaban passes away of unknown causes.

A spatial anomaly is detected by the USS Avalon. The USS Stargazer investigates. A voice demands help from Jean-Luc Picard. Picard is brought aboard and answers, and the message is revealed to be from the Borg, requesting to join the Federation. The Singularity emerges from the anomaly, and a Borg Queen beams aboard the Stargazer and attempts to seize control of the Federation armada assembled there. Picard orders the Stargazer to self-destruct.
A spring vintage of Chateau Picard is bottled at Château Picard.
An offshoot of the Borg are made provisional members of the United Federation of Planets.
Commander Ro Laren dies.
Ensign Eli Foster is revealed to be a rogue Changeling working for Vadic after he is found by Jack Crusher sabotaging the ship.
Fleet Admiral Elizabeth Shelby is killed by her crew.
Following battles in Earth orbit and over Jupiter, the Borg Queen and the Borg Collective are presumed to have been destroyed, freeing a partially assimilated Starfleet from their control.
Following the assimilation of Starfleet by the Borg, the reconstructed Enterprise-D is pressed back into service.
Jack Crusher is revealed to be Jean-Luc Picard's son.
Liam Shaw dies.
Liam Shaw transfers command of the Titan-A to Captain William T. Riker after he is severely injured.
Picard awakens in an altered Château Picard, where he is reunited with Q.
The Borg Collective plots to assimilate Starfleet from within on Frontier Day, using Changelings as infiltrators.
Seven of Nine is promoted to captain.
The 250th anniversary of Starfleet is commemorated with Frontier Day celebrations.
The Enterprise-F is scheduled for early decommissioning.
The Intrepid is sent to rendezvous with the Titan.
The Titan-A is used to rescue Doctor Beverly Crusher and her son Jack from the Ryton system during which they are attacked by Vadic and the Shrike.
The Titan escapes from the Ryton Nebula and heads back to Federation space. Admiral Jean-Luc Picard records an Admiral's Log.
The Titan rendezvous with the Intrepid where most of the Titan’s crew is transported over there.
The Voyager-B is scheduled for launch.
A new version of the Starfleet combadge is introduced between 2401 and this year.
Jack Crusher is assigned to the Enterprise-G as special counselor to the captain.
Raffaela Musiker is assigned to the Enterprise-G as first officer.
Sidney La Forge is promoted to lieutenant junior grade.
The Enterprise-D's restoration is completed, and it is added to the Fleet Museum at Athan Prime.
The Enterprise-G heads out on its shakedown cruise.
By this year, The Doctor had named himself "Joe" in honor of his new wife's grandfather.
In an alternate timeline in which the USS Voyager does not enter the Borg transwarp conduit network and return to Earth in 2378:

Vice Admiral Kathryn Janeway steals a time travel device from Korath, a Klingon scientist belonging to the House of Korath, and travels back in time to 2378 to convince her past self to attempt to return to Earth through the Borg transwarp hub.
In an alternate timeline in which Benjamin Sisko is displaced out of time from 2372, Morn takes over Quark's Bar from Quark, renaming it Morn's.
entities: Quark's Bar, Grill, Gaming House and Holosuite Arcade

events.summary: Norvo Tigan is scheduled to be released from prison.
In an alternate timeline in which Benjamin Sisko is displaced out of time from 2372, he briefly appears in normal time.
Jake Sisko conducts a Q&A session on the subject of his father at Starfleet Academy.

In an alternate timeline in which Alexander Rozhenko chooses to never become a Klingon warrior, Alexander travels back to 2370 from this year in the hopes of convincing his past self to become a warrior, thus avoiding the death of his father, Worf.
A message the Enterprise sent from M-33 in 2364 finally comes within Starfleet's range.

episodes.title: Where No One Has Gone Before

events.summary: The year Katherine Pulaski expected replicative fading to have finished off the Mariposans.
The arrival date Voyager's crew still expected as of 2378.
The year a fifty-year sentence threatened against Quark in 2370 would have run out.
Captain Nog takes the Defiant out as Jake Sisko tries to free his father from displaced time.
Solosos III is reckoned habitable for humans again by now.
Meridian is due back in the prime universe, by Sisko's 2371 reckoning.
Dal R'El, Zero, Maj'el and Jankom Pog trigger a paradox that throws the Protostar into the past.
The USS Protostar arrives here from the late 24th century.
The homecoming date Voyager's crew first expected at the outset of their journey.
Jake Sisko's death breaks the subspace link holding his father, erasing this timeline.
The peaceful death Jadzia Dax forecast for Miles O'Brien back in 2372.
Q's 2372 estimate for when humans would reach the Delta Quadrant.
Barzan II joins the Federation at some point this century.
Starship construction methods unchanged since the 23rd century fall out of use this century.
Mirror Spock's rough forecast for the Halkan-predicted revolt toppling the Terran Empire.
The Federation is said to destroy the Xindi homeworld around this time.
In the future Daniels showed Archer, the Federation breaks the Sphere-Builder invasion at Procyon V.
A neutron star in the Kavis Alpha sector explodes.
A robot ship bound for Kelva in Andromeda is due to arrive.
Fred sees a tan zhekran for the last time before his death in 3191.
A component of the Xindi weapon is built, later sent back to the 22nd century by the Sphere-Builders.
The last recorded contact between the Federation and the Q Continuum.
The earliest the microbrains of Velara III would consider talking to the Federation again.
Kal Dano builds the Tox Uthat, a device capable of stopping fusion in a star.
The Hundred were expected back in the Great Link around now.
Janeway's repairs leave the Nakan memorial powered until this year.
The Doctor adds an ageing program to his matrix.
Decay readings put a probe from the USS Discovery in this era, five centuries ahead of its launch.
Another neutron star explosion in the Kavis Alpha sector.
Federation anthropologists travel back to observe the building of the Great Pyramid at Giza.
Nahla Ake born.
Roughly when the Cerritos would reach Deep Space 6 from Piskes IX at full impulse.
The estimated arrival of the Kelvans' reply to the Federation's message.
In an alternate timeline Henry Starling, flying the stolen timeship Aeon, overshoots into this era.
A third neutron star explosion in the Kavis Alpha sector.
Galactic dilithium begins running out, and the Federation trials other warp designs.
Daniels' temporal database records a hull design commissioned this year.
Daniels' database records two further craft commissioned this year.
A gormagander pup is scanned.
An Earth vessel is lost travelling back to the 22nd century, killing its pilot.
Daniels pulls Archer to this century, and the move wrecks the timeline he came from.
The Khi'eth crashes in the Verubin Nebula while surveying a dilithium nursery.
Starfleet Academy closes, for reasons unrecorded.
The Burn renders almost all galactic dilithium inert, destroying countless ships and lives.
Picard's estimate for how long the core of Atrea IV would stay molten.
The Kyrian historian Quarren finds and revives a backup of The Doctor.
Quarren dies.
The last Federation ship calls at Ni'Var until Discovery's mission a century later.
Deep Space 253 opens trade with an Emerald Chain mercantile.
Ni'Var leaves the Federation.
Starfleet quits Earth, and United Earth quits the Federation.
Federation headquarters moves off Earth.
Titan separates from Earth.
The Federation's last visit to Deep Space 253.
A retrofitted dorsal carrier, design XRT-55D, enters service.
Lura Thok born.
Aditya Sahil begins a watch over a Federation relay station that will last four decades.
Felix murders Patri Doxica's father.
Lura Thok completes the War College.
Caleb Mir born.
Adira Tal born.
Kyheem takes work with the Emerald Chain.
Anisha Mir is sent to a rehabilitation camp as Nus Braka's accomplice.
Caleb Mir escapes the Federation outpost at Pikaru.
Nahla Ake leaves Starfleet.
Nus Braka is sent to a penal colony for stealing rations and killing a supply pilot.
Senna Tal calls anyone still loyal to the Federation to meet him on Earth.
Caleb Mir faces a petty theft charge.
Caleb Mir is charged with destroying property.
Caleb Mir faces a vandalism charge.
Gabrielle Burnham is thrown 950 years forward from 2236 by the Project Daedalus suit.
Admiral Senna Tal dies, the cause unrecorded.
Adira is joined with the Tal symbiont, once carried by Senna and Gray Tal.
Caleb Mir faces a charge of illegal trading.
Michael Burnham arrives from the 23rd century, crashes on Hima, and sends the Red Angel suit back.
Burnham finds Aditya Sahil and commissions him as acting communications chief to help her locate Discovery.
Burnham meets Cleveland Booker and helps him run a trance worm to Sanctuary Four.
Burnham spends a year couriering with Booker while chasing the cause of the Burn.
Adira Tal regains contact with their previous hosts and joins Discovery's crew.
Tried by the Guardian of Forever, Philippa Georgiou departs through its portal.
Nhan leaves Discovery to take command of the USS Tikhov.
Burnham is demoted to science officer for a rogue mission to recover Booker.
Burnham is promoted to captain and given the Discovery.
Saru is confirmed as captain of the USS Discovery.
Saru leaves Starfleet to help Su'Kal make a life on Kaminar.
Sylvia Tilly becomes Discovery's acting first officer.
The Sphere data merges with Discovery's computers, giving rise to the intelligence later called Zora.
Discovery's crew recover Su'Kal, Culber, Saru and Adira from the Khi'eth, and learn Su'Kal caused the Burn.
Discovery's crew break Osyraa and the Emerald Chain.
Discovery defuses the standoff between Wen's raiders and the United Earth Defense Force.
Discovery's crew are reunited with Michael Burnham.
Discovery is formally received back into Starfleet.
Discovery undergoes a three-week retrofit.
The USS Discovery arrives from the 23rd century.
Trill rejoins the Federation.
Zareh is marooned on the Colony.
Starfleet introduces another uniform variant.
Anisha Mir escapes prison.
Archer Spacedock is built.
Caleb Mir is charged with transporting illegal materials.
First contact with Species 10-C.
The Dark Matter Anomaly destroys Kwejian.
Laira Rillak is elected Federation President.
entities: President of the United Federation of Planets

events.summary: Ni'Var rejoins the Federation.
Starfleet Academy reopens.
Discovery delivers dilithium to Alshain IV.
Twenty-one more worlds rejoin, taking Federation membership to fifty-eight.
United Earth rejoins the Federation.
Burnham appoints Rayner her first officer.
Burnham and Saru's last mission together turns up the first clue to the Progenitor technology.
Discovery contends with a Krenim chronophage while tracking Moll and L'ak.
Discovery encounters the ISS Enterprise through a wormhole.
Discovery has a run-in with the Breen.
Discovery's search takes it to Halem'no.
Discovery finds the structure holding the Progenitor technology and leaves it for a later finder.
Discovery calls at the Eternal Gallery and Archive.
Saru accepts a Federation ambassadorship and becomes engaged to T'Rina.
The Bix symbiont, its centuries-long errand done, returns to the Caves of Mak'ala.
